=============================================================================\\\\\\\\n; Use for outbound calls only. Your inbound trunk (peerless_inbound) stays\\\\\\\\n; for incoming calls from the same provider.\\\\\\\\n;\\\\\\\\n; Include in pjsip.conf: #include \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"peerless-outbound.conf\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n; Or merge the sections below into pjsip.conf / pjsip_trunks_custom.conf\\\\\\\\n;\\\\\\\\n; If your provider requires auth: set username/password in the [peerless_outbound]\\\\\\\\n; type=auth section below. When using the app DB sync, set password in config_json\\\\\\\\n; (SuperAdmin → SIP trunks → peerless_outbound) and run config sync.\\\\\\\\n; =============================================================================\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n[peerless_outbound]\\\\\\\\ntype=endpoint\\\\\\\\ntransport=transport-udp\\\\\\\\ncontext=from-trunk\\\\\\\\ndisallow=all\\\\\\\\nallow=ulaw\\\\\\\\nallow=alaw\\\\\\\\noutbound_auth=peerless_outbound\\\\\\\\naors=peerless_outbound\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n[peerless_outbound]\\\\\\\\ntype=auth\\\\\\\\nauth_type=userpass\\\\\\\\nusername=peerless_outbound\\\\\\\\npassword=YOUR_PASSWORD_HERE\\\\\\\\n; realm=208.93.46.221\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n[peerless_outbound]\\\\\\\\ntype=aor\\\\\\\\ncontact=sip:208.93.46.221:5060\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n; Optional: if provider sends calls from this IP (e.g. callbacks), identify so they land in from-trunk\\\\\\\\n[peerless_outbound]\\\\\\\\ntype=identify\\\\\\\\nendpoint=peerless_outbound\\\\\\\\nmatch=208.93.46.221\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"totalLinesInFile\\\\\\\":41}\\\",\\\"toolCall\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"readToolCall\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"args\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"d:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\web development\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\test\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\docs\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\asterisk\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\peerless-outbound.conf\\\\\\\"},\\\\\\\"result\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"success\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"; =============================================================================\\\\\\\\n; Peerless outbound SIP trunk – VoIP provider 208.93.46.221\\\\\\\\n; =============================================================================\\\\\\\\n; Use for outbound calls only. questId\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"attachedFoldersListDirResults\\\":[],\\\"humanChanges\\\":[],\\\"attachedHumanChanges\\\":false,\\\"summarizedComposers\\\":[],\\\"cursorRules\\\":[],\\\"cursorCommands\\\":[],\\\"cursorCommandsExplicitlySet\\\":false,\\\"pastChats\\\":[],\\\"pastChatsExplicitlySet\\\":false,\\\"contextPieces\\\":[],\\\"editTrailContexts\\\":[],\\\"allThinkingBlocks\\\":[],\\\"diffsSinceLastApply\\\":[],\\\"deletedFiles\\\":[],\\\"supportedTools\\\":[],\\\"tokenCount\\\":{\\\"inputTokens\\\":0,\\\"outputTokens\\\":0},\\\"attachedFileCodeChunksMetadataOnly\\\":[],\\\"consoleLogs\\\":[],\\\"uiElementPicked\\\":[],\\\"isRefunded\\\":false,\\\"knowledgeItems\\\":[],\\\"documentationSelections\\\":[],\\\"externalLinks\\\":[],\\\"projectLayouts\\\":[],\\\"unifiedMode\\\":2,\\\"capabilityContexts\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"createdAt\\\":\\\"2026-03-10T10:10:34.967Z\\\",\\\"mcpDescriptors\\\":[],\\\"workspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"conversationState\\\":{\\\"rootPromptMessagesJson\\\":[],\\\"turnsOld\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"pendingToolCalls\\\":[],\\\"turns\\\":[],\\\"previousWorkspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"fileStates\\\":{},\\\"summaryArchives\\\":[],\\\"turnTimings\\\":[],\\\"fileStatesV2\\\":{},\\\"subagentStates\\\":{},\\\"selfSummaryCount\\\":0,\\\"readPaths\\\":[]},\\\"text\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"toolFormerData\\\":{\\\"toolCallId\\\":\\\"tool_f8d4bd03-84f2-4255-ac9f-d9fa0f558b6\\\",\\\"toolIndex\\\":0,\\\"modelCallId\\\":\\\"tool_f8d4bd03-84f2-4255-ac9f-d9fa0f558b6\\\",\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\",\\\"name\\\":\\\"read_file_v2\\\",\\\"rawArgs\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"d:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\web development\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\test\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\docs\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\asterisk\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\README-CALLCENTRE.md\\\\\\\"}\\\",\\\"tool\\\":40,\\\"params\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"targetFile\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"d:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\web development\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\test\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\docs\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\asterisk\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\README-CALLCENTRE.md\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"charsLimit\\\\\\\":1000000,\\\\\\\"effectiveUri\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"d:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\web development\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\test\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\docs\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\asterisk\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\README-CALLCENTRE.md\\\\\\\"}\\\",\\\"result\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"contents\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"# Call Centre – Simple Asterisk Setup\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nTwo files only:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n1. **extensions.conf** – &#96;[globals]&#96; and one include\\\\\\\\n2. **callcentre.conf** – all dialplan (inbound, queue, extension, agent login)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## 1. extensions.conf\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nIn the **&#96;[globals]&#96;** section, add (replace &#96;YOUR_APP_HOST:3001&#96; with your Node app):\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\n[globals]\\\\\\\\n; Call centre app (no trailing slash)\\\\\\\\nAPP_URL=http://YOUR_APP_HOST:3001/api/asterisk/\\\\\\\\nAGENT_LOGIN=${APP_URL}AgentLogin?\\\\\\\\nAGENT_LOGOUT=${APP_URL}AgentLogout?\\\\\\\\nAGENT_LOGIN_SUCCESS=${APP_URL}AgentLoginSuccess?\\\\\\\\nINBOUND_ROUTE=${APP_URL}InboundRoute?\\\\\\\\nQUEUE_MEMBERS=${APP_URL}QueueMembers?\\\\\\\\nCALL_INCOMING=${APP_URL}IncomingCall?\\\\\\\\nCALL_ANSWERED=${APP_URL}CallAnswered?\\\\\\\\nCALL_HANGUP=${APP_URL}CallHangup?\\\\\\\\nRECORDING=${APP_URL}Recording?\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThen include the dialplan file:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\n#include \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"callcentre.conf\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## 2. callcentre.conf\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- Copy **callcentre.conf** from this folder to your Asterisk config directory (e.g. &#96;/etc/asterisk/&#96;).\\\\\\\\n- It contains everything: inbound, WhereToGO, Queue, SipExtension, callstatus, AgentLogin, notify-incoming/answered/hangup, ring-agent, hangup-customer, from-pstn, from-internal.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## 3. Queue calls: dashboard-only (no softphone ring until agent answers)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nFor **queue** calls, the dialplan sends the customer to the Stasis app **queue-dashboard**. The call appears **only** on the agent’s dashboard; the agent’s softphone does **not** ring until the agent clicks **Answer**. Then the app uses ARI to create a bridge and ring the agent’s phone; when they answer, the call is connected.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Requirements:**\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- **ARI** must be enabled on Asterisk and reachable from the Node app (&#96;.env&#96;: &#96;ASTERISK_ARI_URL&#96;, &#96;ASTERISK_ARI_USER&#96;, &#96;ASTERISK_ARI_PASSWORD&#96;).\\\\\\\\n- In **ari.conf**, ensure the Stasis app **queue-dashboard** is allowed (or use the default &#96;allowed_origins&#96; so the Node app can connect to the ARI WebSocket).\\\\\\\\n- The Node app connects to ARI’s WebSocket and handles Stasis events for &#96;queue-dashboard&#96;; no extra Asterisk config is needed beyond ARI.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nAfter a call ends (hangup from dashboard or phone), the agent session stays connected and the agent is ready for the next call.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## 4. What to do on Asterisk\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n1. Set **APP_URL** in &#96;[globals]&#96; to your Node app (e.g. &#96;http://10.0.0.5:3001/api/asterisk/&#96;).\\\\\\\\n2. Point your trunk/DID to context **inbound** (e.g. from your trunk context: &#96;Goto(inbound,s,1)&#96;).\\\\\\\\n3. For agent SIP login, ARI/originate to context **AgentLogin** with channel variables **AgentNumber** and **AgentPassword**.\\\\\\\\n4. Ensure the app database has **inbound_routes**, **queues**, **queue_members**, and **sip_extensions** (or equivalent) configured.\\\\\\\\n5. For queue-dashboard flow, enable ARI and set the Node app’s ARI credentials in &#96;.env&#96;.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Variable names (purpose)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n| Variable | Purpose |\\\\\\\\n|----------|---------|\\\\\\\\n| APP_URL | Base URL of the call centre Node app API |\\\\\\\\n| AGENT_LOGIN | Agent login start (before Authenticate) |\\\\\\\\n| AGENT_LOGOUT | Agent logout / login failed |\\\\\\\\n| AGENT_LOGIN_SUCCESS | Agent login success (after AgentLogin()) |\\\\\\\\n| INBOUND_ROUTE | DID → queue or extension lookup |\\\\\\\\n| QUEUE_MEMBERS | List of queue members for a queue |\\\\\\\\n| CALL_INCOMING | Notify app: call ringing at agent |\\\\\\\\n| CALL_ANSWERED | Notify app: call answered |\\\\\\\\n| CALL_HANGUP | Notify app: call ended |\\\\\\\\n| RECORDING | Notify app: recording path (optional) |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Stasis app queue-dashboard\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- **Queue** calls use &#96;Stasis(queue-dashboard, agentExten, queueName, UNIQUEID)&#96; so the customer is held in the app.\\\\\\\\n- The Node app notifies the dashboard (IncomingCall) and, when the agent clicks **Answer**, creates an ARI bridge, adds the customer, and originates a call to the agent’s PJSIP extension; when the agent answers, the channel is added to the bridge.\\\\\\\\n- **Reject** or **Hangup** from the dashboard hangs up the customer and clears state; the agent remains logged in.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"totalLinesInFile\\\\\\\":90}\\\",\\\"toolCall\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"readToolCall\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"args\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"d:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\web development\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\test\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\docs\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\asterisk\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\README-CALLCENTRE.md\\\\\\\"},\\\\\\\"result\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"success\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"# Call Centre – Simple Asterisk Setup\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nTwo files only:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n1. **extensions.conf** – &#96;[globals]&#96; and one include\\\\\\\\n2. **callcentre.conf** – all dialplan (inbound, queue, extension, agent login)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## 1. extensions.conf\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nIn the **&#96;[globals]&#96;** section, add (replace &#96;YOUR_APP_HOST:3001&#96; with your Node app):\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\n[globals]\\\\\\\\n; Call centre app (no trailing slash)\\\\\\\\nAPP_URL=http://YOUR_APP_HOST:3001/api/asterisk/\\\\\\\\nAGENT_LOGIN=${APP_URL}AgentLogin?\\\\\\\\nAGENT_LOGOUT=${APP_URL}AgentLogout?\\\\\\\\nAGENT_LOGIN_SUCCESS=${APP_URL}AgentLoginSuccess?\\\\\\\\nINBOUND_ROUTE=${APP_URL}InboundRoute?\\\\\\\\nQUEUE_MEMBERS=${APP_URL}QueueMembers?\\\\\\\\nCALL_INCOMING=${APP_URL}IncomingCall?\\\\\\\\nCALL_ANSWERED=${APP_URL}CallAnswered?\\\\\\\\nCALL_HANGUP=${APP_URL}CallHangup?\\\\\\\\nRECORDING=${APP_URL}Recording?\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThen include the dialplan file:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\n#include \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"callcentre.conf\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## 2. callcentre.conf\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- Copy **callcentre.conf** from this folder to your Asterisk config directory (e.g. &#96;/etc/asterisk/&#96;).\\\\\\\\n- It contains everything: inbound, WhereToGO, Queue, SipExtension, callstatus, AgentLogin, notify-incoming/answered/hangup, ring-agent, hangup-customer, from-pstn, from-internal.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## 3. Queue calls: dashboard-only (no softphone ring until agent answers)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nFor **queue** calls, the dialplan sends the customer to the Stasis app **queue-dashboard**. The call appears **only** on the agent’s dashboard; the agent’s softphone does **not** ring until the agent clicks **Answer**. Then the app uses ARI to create a bridge and ring the agent’s phone; when they answer, the call is connected.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Requirements:**\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- **ARI** must be enabled on Asterisk and reachable from the Node app (&#96;.env&#96;: &#96;ASTERISK_ARI_URL&#96;, &#96;ASTERISK_ARI_USER&#96;, &#96;ASTERISK_ARI_PASSWORD&#96;).\\\\\\\\n- In **ari.conf**, ensure the Stasis app **queue-dashboard** is allowed (or use the default &#96;allowed_origins&#96; so the Node app can connect to the ARI WebSocket).\\\\\\\\n- The Node app connects to ARI’s WebSocket and handles Stasis events for &#96;queue-dashboard&#96;; no extra Asterisk config is needed beyond ARI.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nAfter a call ends (hangup from dashboard or phone), the agent session stays connected and the agent is ready for the next call.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## 4. What to do on Asterisk\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n1. Set **APP_URL** in &#96;[globals]&#96; to your Node app (e.g. &#96;http://10.0.0.5:3001/api/asterisk/&#96;).\\\\\\\\n2. Point your trunk/DID to context **inbound** (e.g. from your trunk context: &#96;Goto(inbound,s,1)&#96;).\\\\\\\\n3. For agent SIP login, ARI/originate to context **AgentLogin** with channel variables **AgentNumber** and **AgentPassword**.\\\\\\\\n4. Ensure the app database has **inbound_routes**, **queues**, **queue_members**, and **sip_extensions** (or equivalent) configured.\\\\\\\\n5. For queue-dashboard flow, enable ARI and set the Node app’s ARI credentials in &#96;.env&#96;.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Variable names (purpose)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n| Variable | Purpose |\\\\\\\\n|----------|---------|\\\\\\\\n| APP_URL | Base URL of the call centre Node app API |\\\\\\\\n| AGENT_LOGIN | Agent login start (before Authenticate) |\\\\\\\\n| AGENT_LOGOUT | Agent logout / login failed |\\\\\\\\n| AGENT_LOGIN_SUCCESS | Agent login success (after AgentLogin()) |\\\\\\\\n| INBOUND_ROUTE | DID → queue or extension lookup |\\\\\\\\n| QUEUE_MEMBERS | List of queue members for a queue |\\\\\\\\n| CALL_INCOMING | Notify app: call ringing at agent |\\\\\\\\n| CALL_ANSWERED | Notify app: call answered |\\\\\\\\n| CALL_HANGUP | Notify app: call ended |\\\\\\\\n| RECORDING | Notify app: recording path (optional) |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Stasis app queue-dashboard\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- **Queue** calls use &#96;Stasis(queue-dashboard, agentExten, queueName, UNIQUEID)&#96; so the customer is held in the app.\\\\\\\\n- The Node app notifies the dashboard (IncomingCall) and, when the agent clicks **Answer**, creates an ARI bridge, adds the customer, and originates a call to the agent’s PJSIP extension; when the agent answers, the channel is added to the bridge.\\\\\\\\n- **Reject** or **Hangup** from the dashboard hangs up the customer and clears state; the agent remains logged in.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"totalLines\\\\\\\":90,\\\\\\\"fileSize\\\\\\\":3863,\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"d:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\web development\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\test\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\docs\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\asterisk\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\README-CALLCENTRE.md\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"readRange\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"startLine\\\\\\\":1,\\\\\\\"endLine\\\\\\\":90}}}}}\\\"},\\\"capabilityType\\\":15}\",\"4f2eaf94-0177-4680-a9a8-53e0634ea26f\":\"{\\\"_v\\\":3,\\\"type\\\":2,\\\"approximateLintErrors\\\":[],\\\"lints\\\":[],\\\"codebaseContextChunks\\\":[],\\\"commits\\\":[],\\\"pullRequests\\\":[],\\\"attachedCodeChunks\\\":[],\\\"assistantSuggestedDiffs\\\":[],\\\"gitDiffs\\\":[],\\\"interpreterResults\\\":[],\\\"images\\\":[],\\\"attachedFolders\\\":[],\\\"attachedFoldersNew\\\":[],\\\"bubbleId\\\":\\\"c8e13fba-af6f-4aea-b685-e2085073091f\\\",\\\"userResponsesToSuggestedCodeBlocks\\\":[],\\\"suggestedCodeBlocks\\\":[],\\\"diffsForCompressingFiles\\\":[],\\\"relevantFiles\\\":[],\\\"toolResults\\\":[],\\\"notepads\\\":[],\\\"capabilities\\\":[],\\\"multiFileLinterErrors\\\":[],\\\"diffHistories\\\":[],\\\"recentLocationsHistory\\\":[],\\\"recentlyViewedFiles\\\":[],\\\"isAgentic\\\":false,\\\"fileDiffTrajectories\\\":[],\\\"existedSubsequentTerminalCommand\\\":false,\\\"existedPreviousTerminalCommand\\\":false,\\\"docsReferences\\\":[],\\\"webReferences\\\":[],\\\"aiWebSearchResults\\\":[],\\\"requestId\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"attachedFoldersListDirResults\\\":[],\\\"humanChanges\\\":[],\\\"attachedHumanChanges\\\":false,\\\"summarizedComposers\\\":[],\\\"cursorRules\\\":[],\\\"cursorCommands\\\":[],\\\"cursorCommandsExplicitlySet\\\":false,\\\"pastChats\\\":[],\\\"pastChatsExplicitlySet\\\":false,\\\"contextPieces\\\":[],\\\"editTrailContexts\\\":[],\\\"allThinkingBlocks\\\":[],\\\"diffsSinceLastApply\\\":[],\\\"deletedFiles\\\":[],\\\"supportedTools\\\":[],\\\"tokenCount\\\":{\\\"inputTokens\\\":0,\\\"outputTokens\\\":0},\\\"attachedFileCodeChunksMetadataOnly\\\":[],\\\"consoleLogs\\\":[],\\\"uiElementPicked\\\":[],\\\"isRefunded\\\":false,\\\"knowledgeItems\\\":[],\\\"documentationSelections\\\":[],\\\"externalLinks\\\":[],\\\"projectLayouts\\\":[],\\\"unifiedMode\\\":2,\\\"capabilityContexts\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"createdAt\\\":\\\"2026-03-10T10:10:34.967Z\\\",\\\"mcpDescriptors\\\":[],\\\"workspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"conversationState\\\":{\\\"rootPromptMessagesJson\\\":[],\\\"turnsOld\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"pendingToolCalls\\\":[],\\\"turns\\\":[],\\\"previousWorkspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"fileStates\\\":{},\\\"summaryArchives\\\":[],\\\"turnTimings\\\":[],\\\"fileStatesV2\\\":{},\\\"subagentStates\\\":{},\\\"selfSummaryCount\\\":0,\\\"readPaths\\\":[]},\\\"text\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"toolFormerData\\\":{\\\"toolCallId\\\":\\\"tool_6ce760b1-cb9a-4959-8a41-ce71df68c2b\\\",\\\"toolIndex\\\":0,\\\"modelCallId\\\":\\\"tool_6ce760b1-cb9a-4959-8a41-ce71df68c2b\\\",\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\",\\\"name\\\":\\\"read_file_v2\\\",\\\"rawArgs\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"d:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\web development\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\test\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\docs\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\asterisk\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\README-PBX-CALLCENTRE.md\\\\\\\"}\\\",\\\"tool\\\":40,\\\"params\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"targetFile\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"d:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\web development\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\test\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\docs\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\asterisk\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\README-PBX-CALLCENTRE.md\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"charsLimit\\\\\\\":1000000,\\\\\\\"effectiveUri\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"d:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\web development\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\test\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\docs\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\asterisk\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\README-PBX-CALLCENTRE.md\\\\\\\"}\\\",\\\"result\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"contents\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"# Full dialplan for new app (no us4group.conf)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThis describes the **standalone** dialplan that works with the new Node app only. You do **not** use &#96;us4group.conf&#96; anymore.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Files\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n| File | Purpose |\\\\\\\\n|------|--------|\\\\\\\\n| **pbx-callcentre.conf** | Full dialplan: globals, inbound, queue, extension, AgentLogin, Phase 3. Paste or include this on the Asterisk server. |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Where to paste on the Asterisk server\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Option A – Single include (recommended)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n1. Copy **pbx-callcentre.conf** to the Asterisk config directory:\\\\\\\\n   &#96;&#96;&#96;bash\\\\\\\\n   sudo cp pbx-callcentre.conf /etc/asterisk/\\\\\\\\n   &#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n2. Edit **/etc/asterisk/extensions.conf** and add **one line** (e.g. at the top, after &#96;[general]&#96; if present):\\\\\\\\n   &#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\n   #include \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"pbx-callcentre.conf\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n   &#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n3. In **pbx-callcentre.conf** (on the server), set **APIURL** in the &#96;[globals]&#96; section to your Node app URL, for example:\\\\\\\\n   &#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\n   APIURL=http://10.50.7.181:3001/api/asterisk/\\\\\\\\n   &#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n   Use your app’s host/IP and port (default 3001). No trailing slash.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Option B – You already have [globals] in extensions.conf\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nIf **extensions.conf** already has a &#96;[globals]&#96; section:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n1. Do **not** add another &#96;[globals]&#96; block. Open **pbx-callcentre.conf**, copy **only the variable lines** (from &#96;APIURL=&#96; through &#96;PHASE3_RECORDING=...&#96;), and paste them into the existing &#96;[globals]&#96; in **extensions.conf**. Set **APIURL** to your Node app URL.\\\\\\\\n2. Copy the **rest** of **pbx-callcentre.conf** (from &#96;[inbound]&#96; to the end) into **extensions.conf**, or put that part in a separate file and &#96;#include&#96; it.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Pointing calls at the new dialplan\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- **Inbound from trunk / DID**  \\\\\\\\n  Your trunk or DID must send calls into the **inbound** context.  \\\\\\\\n  Example: in the context that receives the DID (e.g. &#96;from-pstn&#96; or your trunk context), use:\\\\\\\\n  &#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\n  exten =&gt; _X.,1,Goto(inbound,s,1)\\\\\\\\n  &#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n  The dialplan uses **CALLERID(dnid)** as the DID; set it before &#96;Goto(inbound,s,1)&#96; if your trunk doesn’t set it (see &#96;[from-pstn]&#96; in the conf).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- **Agent SIP login**  \\\\\\\\n  The Node app uses ARI to originate a call into the **AgentLogin** context with channel variables **AgentNumber** and **AgentPassword**. No change needed if you already use that.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## What the dialplan does (same idea as old app)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n| Flow | Behaviour |\\\\\\\\n|------|------------|\\\\\\\\n| **Inbound** | Call enters &#96;inbound&#96; → curl **InboundRoute** (DID + caller) → app returns destination (queue or extension) from &#96;inbound_routes&#96; table. |\\\\\\\\n| **WhereToGO** | Branches: 0 = hangup, 2 = Queue, 9 = SipExtension. |\\\\\\\\n| **Queue** | Curl **QueueMembers** to get the list of agents for that queue → try each agent in order with **Phase 3** (IncomingCall → ring → CallAnswered / CallHangup). First to answer gets the call. |\\\\\\\\n| **SipExtension** | Ring a single extension with **Phase 3** (IncomingCall, CallAnswered, CallHangup). |\\\\\\\\n| **AgentLogin** | Answer → curl AgentLogin → Authenticate(AgentPassword) → AgentLogin(AgentNumber) → curl AgentLoginSuccess → Hangup. On hangup before success, curl AgentLogout. |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nSo the new app behaves like the old one: inbound → route by DID → queue (try agents in order) or single extension, plus agent SIP login, all without us4group.conf.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Database (Node app)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- **inbound_routes** – DID, &#96;destination_type&#96; (&#96;queue&#96; or &#96;extension&#96;), &#96;destination_id&#96; (id in &#96;queues&#96; or &#96;sip_extensions&#96;). Used by **InboundRoute**.\\\\\\\\n- **queues** – Queue names. **QueueMembers** is called with &#96;QueueName&#96; (from &#96;queues.name&#96;).\\\\\\\\n- **queue_members** – Which agents are in which queue: &#96;queue_name&#96;, &#96;member_name&#96; (extension number, e.g. 1001). Used by **QueueMembers**.\\\\\\\\n- **sip_extensions** – Extension names/numbers for “inbound to extension”.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## PJSIP vs SIP\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThe dialplan uses **PJSIP** (e.g. &#96;Dial(PJSIP/1001,...)&#96;). If your agents are on **chan_sip**, in **pbx-callcentre.conf** replace **PJSIP/** with **SIP/** in the &#96;Dial()&#96; line inside &#96;[pbx-phase3-ring-one]&#96;.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Reload\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nAfter editing:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\\\\\\\\nsudo asterisk -rx 'dialplan reload'\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"totalLinesInFile\\\\\\\":93}\\\",\\\"toolCall\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"readToolCall\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"args\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"d:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\web development\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\test\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\docs\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\asterisk\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\README-PBX-CALLCENTRE.md\\\\\\\"},\\\\\\\"result\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"success\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"# Full dialplan for new app (no us4group.conf)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThis describes the **standalone** dialplan that works with the new Node app only. You do **not** use &#96;us4group.conf&#96; anymore.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Files\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n| File | Purpose |\\\\\\\\n|------|--------|\\\\\\\\n| **pbx-callcentre.conf** | Full dialplan: globals, inbound, queue, extension, AgentLogin, Phase 3. Paste or include this on the Asterisk server. |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Where to paste on the Asterisk server\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Option A – Single include (recommended)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n1. Copy **pbx-callcentre.conf** to the Asterisk config directory:\\\\\\\\n   &#96;&#96;&#96;bash\\\\\\\\n   sudo cp pbx-callcentre.conf /etc/asterisk/\\\\\\\\n   &#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n2. Edit **/etc/asterisk/extensions.conf** and add **one line** (e.g. at the top, after &#96;[general]&#96; if present):\\\\\\\\n   &#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\n   #include \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"pbx-callcentre.conf\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n   &#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n3. In **pbx-callcentre.conf** (on the server), set **APIURL** in the &#96;[globals]&#96; section to your Node app URL, for example:\\\\\\\\n   &#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\n   APIURL=http://10.50.7.181:3001/api/asterisk/\\\\\\\\n   &#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n   Use your app’s host/IP and port (default 3001). No trailing slash.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Option B – You already have [globals] in extensions.conf\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nIf **extensions.conf** already has a &#96;[globals]&#96; section:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n1. Do **not** add another &#96;[globals]&#96; block. Open **pbx-callcentre.conf**, copy **only the variable lines** (from &#96;APIURL=&#96; through &#96;PHASE3_RECORDING=...&#96;), and paste them into the existing &#96;[globals]&#96; in **extensions.conf**. Set **APIURL** to your Node app URL.\\\\\\\\n2. Copy the **rest** of **pbx-callcentre.conf** (from &#96;[inbound]&#96; to the end) into **extensions.conf**, or put that part in a separate file and &#96;#include&#96; it.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Pointing calls at the new dialplan\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- **Inbound from trunk / DID**  \\\\\\\\n  Your trunk or DID must send calls into the **inbound** context.  \\\\\\\\n  Example: in the context that receives the DID (e.g. &#96;from-pstn&#96; or your trunk context), use:\\\\\\\\n  &#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\n  exten =&gt; _X.,1,Goto(inbound,s,1)\\\\\\\\n  &#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n  The dialplan uses **CALLERID(dnid)** as the DID; set it before &#96;Goto(inbound,s,1)&#96; if your trunk doesn’t set it (see &#96;[from-pstn]&#96; in the conf).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- **Agent SIP login**  \\\\\\\\n  The Node app uses ARI to originate a call into the **AgentLogin** context with channel variables **AgentNumber** and **AgentPassword**. No change needed if you already use that.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## What the dialplan does (same idea as old app)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n| Flow | Behaviour |\\\\\\\\n|------|------------|\\\\\\\\n| **Inbound** | Call enters &#96;inbound&#96; → curl **InboundRoute** (DID + caller) → app returns destination (queue or extension) from &#96;inbound_routes&#96; table. |\\\\\\\\n| **WhereToGO** | Branches: 0 = hangup, 2 = Queue, 9 = SipExtension. |\\\\\\\\n| **Queue** | Curl **QueueMembers** to get the list of agents for that queue → try each agent in order with **Phase 3** (IncomingCall → ring → CallAnswered / CallHangup). First to answer gets the call. |\\\\\\\\n| **SipExtension** | Ring a single extension with **Phase 3** (IncomingCall, CallAnswered, CallHangup). |\\\\\\\\n| **AgentLogin** | Answer → curl AgentLogin → Authenticate(AgentPassword) → AgentLogin(AgentNumber) → curl AgentLoginSuccess → Hangup. On hangup before success, curl AgentLogout. |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nSo the new app behaves like the old one: inbound → route by DID → queue (try agents in order) or single extension, plus agent SIP login, all without us4group.conf.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Database (Node app)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- **inbound_routes** – DID, &#96;destination_type&#96; (&#96;queue&#96; or &#96;extension&#96;), &#96;destination_id&#96; (id in &#96;queues&#96; or &#96;sip_extensions&#96;). Used by **InboundRoute**.\\\\\\\\n- **queues** – Queue names. **QueueMembers** is called with &#96;QueueName&#96; (from &#96;queues.name&#96;).\\\\\\\\n- **queue_members** – Which agents are in which queue: &#96;queue_name&#96;, &#96;member_name&#96; (extension number, e.g. 1001). Used by **QueueMembers**.\\\\\\\\n- **sip_extensions** – Extension names/numbers for “inbound to extension”.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## PJSIP vs SIP\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThe dialplan uses **PJSIP** (e.g. &#96;Dial(PJSIP/1001,...)&#96;). If your agents are on **chan_sip**, in **pbx-callcentre.conf** replace **PJSIP/** with **SIP/** in the &#96;Dial()&#96; line inside &#96;[pbx-phase3-ring-one]&#96;.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Reload\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nAfter editing:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\\\\\\\\nsudo asterisk -rx 'dialplan reload'\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"totalLines\\\\\\\":93,\\\\\\\"fileSize\\\\\\\":4037,\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"d:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\web development\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\test\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\docs\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\asterisk\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\README-PBX-CALLCENTRE.md\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"readRange\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"startLine\\\\\\\":1,\\\\\\\"endLine\\\\\\\":93}}}}}\\\"},\\\"capabilityType\\\":15}\",\"2c100572-b84b-4808-ba3a-008b6a12ae40\":\"{\\\"_v\\\":3,\\\"type\\\":2,\\\"approximateLintErrors\\\":[],\\\"lints\\\":[],\\\"codebaseContextChunks\\\":[],\\\"commits\\\":[],\\\"pullRequests\\\":[],\\\"attachedCodeChunks\\\":[],\\\"assistantSuggestedDiffs\\\":[],\\\"gitDiffs\\\":[],\\\"interpreterResults\\\":[],\\\"images\\\":[],\\\"attachedFolders\\\":[],\\\"attachedFoldersNew\\\":[],\\\"bubbleId\\\":\\\"2e7d1487-9866-4bb6-bf8b-bbc22525462e\\\",\\\"userResponsesToSuggestedCodeBlocks\\\":[],\\\"suggestedCodeBlocks\\\":[],\\\"diffsForCompressingFiles\\\":[],\\\"relevantFiles\\\":[],\\\"toolResults\\\":[],\\\"notepads\\\":[],\\\"capabilities\\\":[],\\\"multiFileLinterErrors\\\":[],\\\"diffHistories\\\":[],\\\"recentLocationsHistory\\\":[],\\\"recentlyViewedFiles\\\":[],\\\"isAgentic\\\":false,\\\"fileDiffTrajectories\\\":[],\\\"existedSubsequentTerminalCommand\\\":false,\\\"existedPreviousTerminalCommand\\\":false,\\\"docsReferences\\\":[],\\\"webReferences\\\":[],\\\"aiWebSearchResults\\\":[],\\\"requestId\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"attachedFoldersListDirResults\\\":[],\\\"humanChanges\\\":[],\\\"attachedHumanChanges\\\":false,\\\"summarizedComposers\\\":[],\\\"cursorRules\\\":[],\\\"cursorCommands\\\":[],\\\"cursorCommandsExplicitlySet\\\":false,\\\"pastChats\\\":[],\\\"pastChatsExplicitlySet\\\":false,\\\"contextPieces\\\":[],\\\"editTrailContexts\\\":[],\\\"allThinkingBlocks\\\":[],\\\"diffsSinceLastApply\\\":[],\\\"deletedFiles\\\":[],\\\"supportedTools\\\":[],\\\"tokenCount\\\":{\\\"inputTokens\\\":0,\\\"outputTokens\\\":0},\\\"attachedFileCodeChunksMetadataOnly\\\":[],\\\"consoleLogs\\\":[],\\\"uiElementPicked\\\":[],\\\"isRefunded\\\":false,\\\"knowledgeItems\\\":[],\\\"documentationSelections\\\":[],\\\"externalLinks\\\":[],\\\"projectLayouts\\\":[],\\\"unifiedMode\\\":2,\\\"capabilityContexts\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"createdAt\\\":\\\"2026-03-10T10:10:34.968Z\\\",\\\"mcpDescriptors\\\":[],\\\"workspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"conversationState\\\":{\\\"rootPromptMessagesJson\\\":[],\\\"turnsOld\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"pendingToolCalls\\\":[],\\\"turns\\\":[],\\\"previousWorkspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"fileStates\\\":{},\\\"summaryArchives\\\":[],\\\"turnTimings\\\":[],\\\"fileStatesV2\\\":{},\\\"subagentStates\\\":{},\\\"selfSummaryCount\\\":0,\\\"readPaths\\\":[]},\\\"text\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"toolFormerData\\\":{\\\"toolCallId\\\":\\\"tool_423d56b5-d643-4f80-be62-2c73b05e37e\\\",\\\"toolIndex\\\":0,\\\"modelCallId\\\":\\\"tool_423d56b5-d643-4f80-be62-2c73b05e37e\\\",\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\",\\\"name\\\":\\\"read_file_v2\\\",\\\"rawArgs\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"d:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\web development\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\test\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\docs\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\asterisk\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\README-TWO-SETUPS.md\\\\\\\"}\\\",\\\"tool\\\":40,\\\"params\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"targetFile\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"d:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\web development\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\test\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\docs\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\asterisk\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\README-TWO-SETUPS.md\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"charsLimit\\\\\\\":1000000,\\\\\\\"effectiveUri\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"d:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\web development\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\test\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\docs\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\asterisk\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\README-TWO-SETUPS.md\\\\\\\"}\\\",\\\"result\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"contents\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"# Two ways to use the dialplan\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nYou can either **keep your current files** and add only the inbound/queue flow, or use **one full file**. Pick one.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Option 1: Keep your current setup (recommended if you already have it)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nYou already have:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- **[globals]** in **extensions.conf** (with APIURL, Phase 3 URLs, etc.)\\\\\\\\n- **#include \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"pbx.conf\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"** (with AgentLogin)\\\\\\\\n- **#include \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"phase3-dialplan.conf\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"** (Phase 3 subroutines)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Keep all of that as-is.** Then:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### 1. Add two variables to [globals] in extensions.conf\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nAdd these two lines to your existing &#96;[globals]&#96; section (if not already there):\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\nINBOUND_ROUTE=${APIURL}InboundRoute?\\\\\\\\nQUEUE_MEMBERS=${APIURL}QueueMembers?\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nSo your globals might look like:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\n[globals]\\\\\\\\nAPIURL=http://YOUR_APP_HOST:3001/api/asterisk/\\\\\\\\nUS4GROUP_AgentLogin=${APIURL}US4GROUP_Agent/AgentLogin?\\\\\\\\nUS4GROUP_AgentLogout=${APIURL}US4GROUP_Agent/AgentLogout?\\\\\\\\nUS4GROUP_AgentLoginSuccess=${APIURL}US4GROUP_Agent/AgentLoginSuccess?\\\\\\\\nPHASE3_INCOMING_CALL=${APIURL}IncomingCall?\\\\\\\\nPHASE3_CALL_ANSWERED=${APIURL}CallAnswered?\\\\\\\\nPHASE3_CALL_HANGUP=${APIURL}CallHangup?\\\\\\\\nPHASE3_RECORDING=${APIURL}Recording?\\\\\\\\nINBOUND_ROUTE=${APIURL}InboundRoute?\\\\\\\\nQUEUE_MEMBERS=${APIURL}QueueMembers?\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### 2. Add the inbound/queue/extension file\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- Copy **pbx-inbound.conf** to the Asterisk server (e.g. &#96;/etc/asterisk/pbx-inbound.conf&#96;).\\\\\\\\n- In **extensions.conf** add one more include (order can be after phase3-dialplan.conf):\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\n#include \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"phase3-dialplan.conf\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n#include \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"pbx-inbound.conf\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Do not** include **pbx-callcentre.conf** here; that would duplicate AgentLogin and Phase 3.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### 3. Point inbound calls to the new flow\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nPoint your trunk/DID to context **inbound** (e.g. in the context that receives the call):\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\nexten =&gt; _X.,1,Goto(inbound,s,1)\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Summary for Option 1:**\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n| File / place | Role |\\\\\\\\n|--------------|------|\\\\\\\\n| **extensions.conf** | [globals] + &#96;#include \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"pbx.conf\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"&#96; + &#96;#include \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"phase3-dialplan.conf\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"&#96; + &#96;#include \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"pbx-inbound.conf\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"&#96;. Add INBOUND_ROUTE and QUEUE_MEMBERS to [globals]. |\\\\\\\\n| **pbx.conf** | Keep as-is (AgentLogin). |\\\\\\\\n| **phase3-dialplan.conf** | Keep as-is (Phase 3 subroutines). |\\\\\\\\n| **pbx-inbound.conf** | New: [inbound], [WhereToGO], [Queue], [SipExtension], [callstatus], [from-pstn], [from-internal]. |\\\\\\\\n| **pbx-callcentre.conf** | Do **not** use in this setup. |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Option 2: One file only (no pbx.conf / phase3-dialplan.conf)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nIf you prefer a single dialplan file and are **not** using the split setup above:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- Use **only pbx-callcentre.conf** (it has [globals], AgentLogin, Phase 3, and inbound/queue/extension).\\\\\\\\n- In **extensions.conf** have only:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\n#include \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"pbx-callcentre.conf\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- Do **not** include pbx.conf or phase3-dialplan.conf (everything is in pbx-callcentre.conf).\\\\\\\\n- Set **APIURL** inside the [globals] in pbx-callcentre.conf (or merge those variables into your existing [globals] and remove the [globals] block from pbx-callcentre.conf).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Quick decision\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- **Already using globals + pbx.conf + phase3-dialplan.conf** → stay with them, add **INBOUND_ROUTE** and **QUEUE_MEMBERS** to globals, and add **pbx-inbound.conf** (Option 1). Do **not** add pbx-callcentre.conf.\\\\\\\\n- **Starting fresh or want one file** → use **pbx-callcentre.conf** only and do not include pbx.conf or phase3-dialplan.conf (Option 2).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"totalLinesInFile\\\\\\\":94}\\\",\\\"toolCall\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"readToolCall\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"args\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"d:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\web development\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\test\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\docs\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\asterisk\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\README-TWO-SETUPS.md\\\\\\\"},\\\\\\\"result\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"success\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"# Two ways to use the dialplan\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nYou can either **keep your current files** and add only the inbound/queue flow, or use **one full file**. Pick one.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Option 1: Keep your current setup (recommended if you already have it)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nYou already have:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- **[globals]** in **extensions.conf** (with APIURL, Phase 3 URLs, etc.)\\\\\\\\n- **#include \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"pbx.conf\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"** (with AgentLogin)\\\\\\\\n- **#include \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"phase3-dialplan.conf\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"** (Phase 3 subroutines)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Keep all of that as-is.** Then:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### 1. Add two variables to [globals] in extensions.conf\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nAdd these two lines to your existing &#96;[globals]&#96; section (if not already there):\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\nINBOUND_ROUTE=${APIURL}InboundRoute?\\\\\\\\nQUEUE_MEMBERS=${APIURL}QueueMembers?\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nSo your globals might look like:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\n[globals]\\\\\\\\nAPIURL=http://YOUR_APP_HOST:3001/api/asterisk/\\\\\\\\nUS4GROUP_AgentLogin=${APIURL}US4GROUP_Agent/AgentLogin?\\\\\\\\nUS4GROUP_AgentLogout=${APIURL}US4GROUP_Agent/AgentLogout?\\\\\\\\nUS4GROUP_AgentLoginSuccess=${APIURL}US4GROUP_Agent/AgentLoginSuccess?\\\\\\\\nPHASE3_INCOMING_CALL=${APIURL}IncomingCall?\\\\\\\\nPHASE3_CALL_ANSWERED=${APIURL}CallAnswered?\\\\\\\\nPHASE3_CALL_HANGUP=${APIURL}CallHangup?\\\\\\\\nPHASE3_RECORDING=${APIURL}Recording?\\\\\\\\nINBOUND_ROUTE=${APIURL}InboundRoute?\\\\\\\\nQUEUE_MEMBERS=${APIURL}QueueMembers?\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### 2. Add the inbound/queue/extension file\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- Copy **pbx-inbound.conf** to the Asterisk server (e.g. &#96;/etc/asterisk/pbx-inbound.conf&#96;).\\\\\\\\n- In **extensions.conf** add one more include (order can be after phase3-dialplan.conf):\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\n#include \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"phase3-dialplan.conf\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n#include \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"pbx-inbound.conf\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Do not** include **pbx-callcentre.conf** here; that would duplicate AgentLogin and Phase 3.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### 3. Point inbound calls to the new flow\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nPoint your trunk/DID to context **inbound** (e.g. in the context that receives the call):\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\nexten =&gt; _X.,1,Goto(inbound,s,1)\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Summary for Option 1:**\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n| File / place | Role |\\\\\\\\n|--------------|------|\\\\\\\\n| **extensions.conf** | [globals] + &#96;#include \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"pbx.conf\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"&#96; + &#96;#include \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"phase3-dialplan.conf\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"&#96; + &#96;#include \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"pbx-inbound.conf\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"&#96;. Add INBOUND_ROUTE and QUEUE_MEMBERS to [globals]. |\\\\\\\\n| **pbx.conf** | Keep as-is (AgentLogin). |\\\\\\\\n| **phase3-dialplan.conf** | Keep as-is (Phase 3 subroutines). |\\\\\\\\n| **pbx-inbound.conf** | New: [inbound], [WhereToGO], [Queue], [SipExtension], [callstatus], [from-pstn], [from-internal]. |\\\\\\\\n| **pbx-callcentre.conf** | Do **not** use in this setup. |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Option 2: One file only (no pbx.conf / phase3-dialplan.conf)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nIf you prefer a single dialplan file and are **not** using the split setup above:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- Use **only pbx-callcentre.conf** (it has [globals], AgentLogin, Phase 3, and inbound/queue/extension).\\\\\\\\n- In **extensions.conf** have only:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\n#include \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"pbx-callcentre.conf\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- Do **not** include pbx.conf or phase3-dialplan.conf (everything is in pbx-callcentre.conf).\\\\\\\\n- Set **APIURL** inside the [globals] in pbx-callcentre.conf (or merge those variables into your existing [globals] and remove the [globals] block from pbx-callcentre.conf).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Quick decision\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- **Already using globals + pbx.conf + phase3-dialplan.conf** → stay with them, add **INBOUND_ROUTE** and **QUEUE_MEMBERS** to globals, and add **pbx-inbound.conf** (Option 1). Do **not** add pbx-callcentre.conf.\\\\\\\\n- **Starting fresh or want one file** → use **pbx-callcentre.conf** only and do not include pbx.conf or phase3-dialplan.conf (Option 2).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"totalLines\\\\\\\":94,\\\\\\\"fileSize\\\\\\\":3321,\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"d:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\web development\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\test\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\docs\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\asterisk\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\README-TWO-SETUPS.md\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"readRange\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"startLine\\\\\\\":1,\\\\\\\"endLine\\\\\\\":94}}}}}\\\"},\\\"capabilityType\\\":15}\",\"3a1d6352-45a5-450d-8247-0515d81bcf03\":\"{\\\"_v\\\":3,\\\"type\\\":2,\\\"approximateLintErrors\\\":[],\\\"lints\\\":[],\\\"codebaseContextChunks\\\":[],\\\"commits\\\":[],\\\"pullRequests\\\":[],\\\"attachedCodeChunks\\\":[],\\\"assistantSuggestedDiffs\\\":[],\\\"gitDiffs\\\":[],\\\"interpreterResults\\\":[],\\\"images\\\":[],\\\"attachedFolders\\\":[],\\\"attachedFoldersNew\\\":[],\\\"bubbleId\\\":\\\"8664af1e-c3fe-405b-8c36-90f9dc2f0d85\\\",\\\"userResponsesToSuggestedCodeBlocks\\\":[],\\\"suggestedCodeBlocks\\\":[],\\\"diffsForCompressingFiles\\\":[],\\\"relevantFiles\\\":[],\\\"toolResults\\\":[],\\\"notepads\\\":[],\\\"capabilities\\\":[],\\\"multiFileLinterErrors\\\":[],\\\"diffHistories\\\":[],\\\"recentLocationsHistory\\\":[],\\\"recentlyViewedFiles\\\":[],\\\"isAgentic\\\":false,\\\"fileDiffTrajectories\\\":[],\\\"existedSubsequentTerminalCommand\\\":false,\\\"existedPreviousTerminalCommand\\\":false,\\\"docsReferences\\\":[],\\\"webReferences\\\":[],\\\"aiWebSearchResults\\\":[],\\\"requestId\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"attachedFoldersListDirResults\\\":[],\\\"humanChanges\\\":[],\\\"attachedHumanChanges\\\":false,\\\"summarizedComposers\\\":[],\\\"cursorRules\\\":[],\\\"cursorCommands\\\":[],\\\"cursorCommandsExplicitlySet\\\":false,\\\"pastChats\\\":[],\\\"pastChatsExplicitlySet\\\":false,\\\"contextPieces\\\":[],\\\"editTrailContexts\\\":[],\\\"allThinkingBlocks\\\":[],\\\"diffsSinceLastApply\\\":[],\\\"deletedFiles\\\":[],\\\"supportedTools\\\":[],\\\"tokenCount\\\":{\\\"inputTokens\\\":0,\\\"outputTokens\\\":0},\\\"attachedFileCodeChunksMetadataOnly\\\":[],\\\"consoleLogs\\\":[],\\\"uiElementPicked\\\":[],\\\"isRefunded\\\":false,\\\"knowledgeItems\\\":[],\\\"documentationSelections\\\":[],\\\"externalLinks\\\":[],\\\"projectLayouts\\\":[],\\\"unifiedMode\\\":2,\\\"capabilityContexts\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"createdAt\\\":\\\"2026-03-10T10:10:34.969Z\\\",\\\"mcpDescriptors\\\":[],\\\"workspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"conversationState\\\":{\\\"rootPromptMessagesJson\\\":[],\\\"turnsOld\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"pendingToolCalls\\\":[],\\\"turns\\\":[],\\\"previousWorkspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"fileStates\\\":{},\\\"summaryArchives\\\":[],\\\"turnTimings\\\":[],\\\"fileStatesV2\\\":{},\\\"subagentStates\\\":{},\\\"selfSummaryCount\\\":0,\\\"readPaths\\\":[]},\\\"text\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"toolFormerData\\\":{\\\"toolCallId\\\":\\\"tool_0d99de56-3b4d-40d9-ba25-00e39bbfce3\\\",\\\"toolIndex\\\":0,\\\"modelCallId\\\":\\\"tool_0d99de56-3b4d-40d9-ba25-00e39bbfce3\\\",\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\",\\\"name\\\":\\\"read_file_v2\\\",\\\"rawArgs\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"d:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\web development\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\test\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\docs\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\asterisk\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\README-PHASE3-DIALPLAN.md\\\\\\\"}\\\",\\\"tool\\\":40,\\\"params\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"targetFile\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"d:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\web development\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\test\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\docs\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\asterisk\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\README-PHASE3-DIALPLAN.md\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"charsLimit\\\\\\\":1000000,\\\\\\\"effectiveUri\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"d:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\web development\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\test\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\docs\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\asterisk\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\README-PHASE3-DIALPLAN.md\\\\\\\"}\\\",\\\"result\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"contents\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"# Phase 3 Asterisk dialplan – where to paste\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThis folder contains dialplan snippets for **Phase 3 real-time call handling**. The Asterisk server must be able to reach your Node app over HTTP (e.g. &#96;http://YOUR_APP_HOST:3001&#96;).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## 1. Where Asterisk keeps dialplan\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nOn a typical Linux Asterisk server:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n| File / directory | Purpose |\\\\\\\\n|------------------|--------|\\\\\\\\n| **&#96;/etc/asterisk/extensions.conf&#96;** | Main dialplan. You add &#96;#include&#96; and/or paste &#96;[globals]&#96; lines here. |\\\\\\\\n| **&#96;/etc/asterisk/&#96;** | Other config (e.g. &#96;pjsip.conf&#96;, &#96;queues.conf&#96;). |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nSo:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- **Globals** → add the Phase 3 variables into the **&#96;[globals]&#96;** section of **&#96;/etc/asterisk/extensions.conf&#96;**.\\\\\\\\n- **Dialplan** → either paste the Phase 3 contexts into **&#96;/etc/asterisk/extensions.conf&#96;**, or put them in a separate file and **&#96;#include&#96;** that file from &#96;extensions.conf&#96;.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## 2. Step-by-step placement\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Step 2.1 – Add Phase 3 globals\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nOpen **&#96;/etc/asterisk/extensions.conf&#96;** and find the **&#96;[globals]&#96;** section. Add (or merge) these lines. Replace &#96;http://YOUR_APP_HOST:3001&#96; with the URL of your Node app (host/IP and port) so Asterisk can reach it:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\n[globals]\\\\\\\\n; ... your existing globals (APIURL, US4GROUP_AgentLogin, etc.) ...\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n; Phase 3 – Node app base URL (no trailing slash)\\\\\\\\nAPIURL=http://YOUR_APP_HOST:3001/api/asterisk/\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n; Phase 3 call event URLs (used by phase3-dialplan.conf)\\\\\\\\nPHASE3_INCOMING_CALL=${APIURL}IncomingCall?\\\\\\\\nPHASE3_CALL_ANSWERED=${APIURL}CallAnswered?\\\\\\\\nPHASE3_CALL_HANGUP=${APIURL}CallHangup?\\\\\\\\nPHASE3_RECORDING=${APIURL}Recording?\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nExample: if the app runs on &#96;10.50.7.181&#96; port &#96;3001&#96;:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\nAPIURL=http://10.50.7.181:3001/api/asterisk/\\\\\\\\nPHASE3_INCOMING_CALL=${APIURL}IncomingCall?\\\\\\\\nPHASE3_CALL_ANSWERED=${APIURL}CallAnswered?\\\\\\\\nPHASE3_CALL_HANGUP=${APIURL}CallHangup?\\\\\\\\nPHASE3_RECORDING=${APIURL}Recording?\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nIf you already have &#96;APIURL&#96; for the new app (e.g. from ASTERISK-AGENT-LOGIN-SETUP.md), add only the &#96;PHASE3_*&#96; lines.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Step 2.2 – Add Phase 3 dialplan\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Option A – Include a separate file (recommended)**\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n1. Copy **&#96;phase3-dialplan.conf&#96;** from this repo to the Asterisk server, for example:\\\\\\\\n   &#96;&#96;&#96;bash\\\\\\\\n   sudo cp phase3-dialplan.conf /etc/asterisk/\\\\\\\\n   &#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n2. In **&#96;/etc/asterisk/extensions.conf&#96;**, add an include **after** the &#96;[globals]&#96; section and **before** any context that uses Phase 3 (e.g. before &#96;#include us4group.conf&#96; if you use it):\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n   &#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\n   #include \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"phase3-dialplan.conf\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n   &#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Option B – Paste into extensions.conf**\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n1. Open **&#96;phase3-dialplan.conf&#96;** from this repo.\\\\\\\\n2. Copy its contents (from &#96;[pbx-phase3-incoming]&#96; to the end of the file).\\\\\\\\n3. Paste them into **&#96;/etc/asterisk/extensions.conf&#96;** after &#96;[globals]&#96; and before your main contexts (e.g. before &#96;[US4GROUP]&#96; or &#96;#include us4group.conf&#96;).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Step 2.3 – Use Phase 3 when ringing an agent\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nWhere you currently **ring a single agent** (e.g. “inbound to extension” or “ring agent 1001”), call the Phase 3 subroutine instead of (or in addition to) a plain &#96;Dial()&#96;.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Example – ring one agent with Phase 3 (e.g. from your inbound flow):**\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\n; Agent extension number (e.g. 1001) and optional queue name\\\\\\\\nSet(PHASE3_AGENT_ID=1001)\\\\\\\\nSet(PHASE3_QUEUE_NAME=Support)\\\\\\\\nGosub(pbx-phase3-ring-one,s,1(${PHASE3_AGENT_ID},${PHASE3_QUEUE_NAME}))\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nSo:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- **Where to paste this:** in the place in **&#96;/etc/asterisk/extensions.conf&#96;** (or in **&#96;us4group.conf&#96;** if you &#96;#include&#96; it) where you today do something like &#96;Dial(SIP/1001,...)&#96; or &#96;GoSub(...,SipExtension,...)&#96; for a **single** agent. Replace that with the three lines above (with the right agent id and queue name), or call &#96;Gosub(pbx-phase3-ring-one,s,1(1001,Support))&#96; with your own variables.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Example – replace “SipExtension” with Phase 3 (in us4group.conf or equivalent):**\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nIf you have something like:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\nexten =&gt; SipExtension,1,...\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n,Dial(SIP/${ExtensionNumber},...)\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nyou can change it to:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\nexten =&gt; SipExtension,1,...\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n,Gosub(pbx-phase3-ring-one,s,1(${ExtensionNumber},Inbound))\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n,Return()\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n(Adjust &#96;ExtensionNumber&#96; / queue name to match your variables.)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Step 2.4 – Optional: Recording callback\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nTo send the recording path to the Node app when a call is recorded:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- After **MixMonitor** or **Record**, when you have the file path and the call’s **UniqueID**, call the Recording URL, e.g. from a shell script or from the dialplan:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  &#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\n  Set(PHASE3_REC_PATH=${URIENCODE(${RECORDED_FILE})})\\\\\\\\n  Set(PHASE3_REC_RESULT=${SHELL(curl -s \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"${PHASE3_RECORDING}UniqueID=${UNIQUEID}&amp;Path=${PHASE3_REC_PATH}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")})\\\\\\\\n  &#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- Or from the command line (for testing):\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  &#96;&#96;&#96;bash\\\\\\\\n  curl -s \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"http://YOUR_APP_HOST:3001/api/asterisk/Recording?UniqueID=UNIQUEID&amp;Path=/path/to/file.wav\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n  &#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## 3. Reload and test\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nOn the Asterisk server:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\\\\\\\\nsudo asterisk -rx 'dialplan reload'\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nFrom the Asterisk server, test that it can reach the app:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\\\\\\\\ncurl -s \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"http://YOUR_APP_HOST:3001/api/asterisk/IncomingCall?AgentID=1001&amp;UniqueID=test123&amp;ChannelID=ch1&amp;CustomerChannelID=ch2&amp;CustomerNumber=15551234567&amp;QueueName=Support\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nYou should get a JSON response like &#96;{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"ok\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":true}&#96;.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## 4. File summary\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n| File in repo | Purpose | Where it goes on Asterisk |\\\\\\\\n|--------------|--------|----------------------------|\\\\\\\\n| **phase3-globals.conf** | Reference for globals to add | Copy the lines into &#96;[globals]&#96; in &#96;/etc/asterisk/extensions.conf&#96; |\\\\\\\\n| **phase3-dialplan.conf** | Phase 3 contexts and subroutines | Copy to &#96;/etc/asterisk/phase3-dialplan.conf&#96; and &#96;#include \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"phase3-dialplan.conf\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"&#96; in &#96;extensions.conf&#96;, or paste contents into &#96;extensions.conf&#96; |\\\\\\\\n| **phase3-example-inbound-extension.conf** | Example “inbound to extension” with Phase 3 | Optional; use as reference or include if you want that example |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## 5. PJSIP vs SIP\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThe Phase 3 dialplan uses **PJSIP** (e.g. &#96;Dial(PJSIP/1001,...)&#96;). If your agents are registered as **chan_sip** (SIP), change in **phase3-dialplan.conf**:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- Replace **&#96;PJSIP/&#96;** with **&#96;SIP/&#96;** in the &#96;Dial()&#96; line in &#96;[pbx-phase3-ring-one]&#96;.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"totalLinesInFile\\\\\\\":169}\\\",\\\"toolCall\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"readToolCall\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"args\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"d:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\web development\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\test\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\docs\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\asterisk\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\README-PHASE3-DIALPLAN.md\\\\\\\"},\\\\\\\"result\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"success\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"# Phase 3 Asterisk dialplan – where to paste\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThis folder contains dialplan snippets for **Phase 3 real-time call handling**. The Asterisk server must be able to reach your Node app over HTTP (e.g. &#96;http://YOUR_APP_HOST:3001&#96;).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## 1. Where Asterisk keeps dialplan\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nOn a typical Linux Asterisk server:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n| File / directory | Purpose |\\\\\\\\n|------------------|--------|\\\\\\\\n| **&#96;/etc/asterisk/extensions.conf&#96;** | Main dialplan. You add &#96;#include&#96; and/or paste &#96;[globals]&#96; lines here. |\\\\\\\\n| **&#96;/etc/asterisk/&#96;** | Other config (e.g. &#96;pjsip.conf&#96;, &#96;queues.conf&#96;). |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nSo:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- **Globals** → add the Phase 3 variables into the **&#96;[globals]&#96;** section of **&#96;/etc/asterisk/extensions.conf&#96;**.\\\\\\\\n- **Dialplan** → either paste the Phase 3 contexts into **&#96;/etc/asterisk/extensions.conf&#96;**, or put them in a separate file and **&#96;#include&#96;** that file from &#96;extensions.conf&#96;.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## 2. Step-by-step placement\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Step 2.1 – Add Phase 3 globals\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nOpen **&#96;/etc/asterisk/extensions.conf&#96;** and find the **&#96;[globals]&#96;** section. Add (or merge) these lines. Replace &#96;http://YOUR_APP_HOST:3001&#96; with the URL of your Node app (host/IP and port) so Asterisk can reach it:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\n[globals]\\\\\\\\n; ... your existing globals (APIURL, US4GROUP_AgentLogin, etc.) ...\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n; Phase 3 – Node app base URL (no trailing slash)\\\\\\\\nAPIURL=http://YOUR_APP_HOST:3001/api/asterisk/\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n; Phase 3 call event URLs (used by phase3-dialplan.conf)\\\\\\\\nPHASE3_INCOMING_CALL=${APIURL}IncomingCall?\\\\\\\\nPHASE3_CALL_ANSWERED=${APIURL}CallAnswered?\\\\\\\\nPHASE3_CALL_HANGUP=${APIURL}CallHangup?\\\\\\\\nPHASE3_RECORDING=${APIURL}Recording?\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nExample: if the app runs on &#96;10.50.7.181&#96; port &#96;3001&#96;:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\nAPIURL=http://10.50.7.181:3001/api/asterisk/\\\\\\\\nPHASE3_INCOMING_CALL=${APIURL}IncomingCall?\\\\\\\\nPHASE3_CALL_ANSWERED=${APIURL}CallAnswered?\\\\\\\\nPHASE3_CALL_HANGUP=${APIURL}CallHangup?\\\\\\\\nPHASE3_RECORDING=${APIURL}Recording?\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nIf you already have &#96;APIURL&#96; for the new app (e.g. from ASTERISK-AGENT-LOGIN-SETUP.md), add only the &#96;PHASE3_*&#96; lines.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Step 2.2 – Add Phase 3 dialplan\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Option A – Include a separate file (recommended)**\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n1. Copy **&#96;phase3-dialplan.conf&#96;** from this repo to the Asterisk server, for example:\\\\\\\\n   &#96;&#96;&#96;bash\\\\\\\\n   sudo cp phase3-dialplan.conf /etc/asterisk/\\\\\\\\n   &#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n2. In **&#96;/etc/asterisk/extensions.conf&#96;**, add an include **after** the &#96;[globals]&#96; section and **before** any context that uses Phase 3 (e.g. before &#96;#include us4group.conf&#96; if you use it):\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n   &#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\n   #include \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"phase3-dialplan.conf\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n   &#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Option B – Paste into extensions.conf**\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n1. Open **&#96;phase3-dialplan.conf&#96;** from this repo.\\\\\\\\n2. Copy its contents (from &#96;[pbx-phase3-incoming]&#96; to the end of the file).\\\\\\\\n3. Paste them into **&#96;/etc/asterisk/extensions.conf&#96;** after &#96;[globals]&#96; and before your main contexts (e.g. before &#96;[US4GROUP]&#96; or &#96;#include us4group.conf&#96;).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Step 2.3 – Use Phase 3 when ringing an agent\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nWhere you currently **ring a single agent** (e.g. “inbound to extension” or “ring agent 1001”), call the Phase 3 subroutine instead of (or in addition to) a plain &#96;Dial()&#96;.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Example – ring one agent with Phase 3 (e.g. from your inbound flow):**\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\n; Agent extension number (e.g. 1001) and optional queue name\\\\\\\\nSet(PHASE3_AGENT_ID=1001)\\\\\\\\nSet(PHASE3_QUEUE_NAME=Support)\\\\\\\\nGosub(pbx-phase3-ring-one,s,1(${PHASE3_AGENT_ID},${PHASE3_QUEUE_NAME}))\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nSo:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- **Where to paste this:** in the place in **&#96;/etc/asterisk/extensions.conf&#96;** (or in **&#96;us4group.conf&#96;** if you &#96;#include&#96; it) where you today do something like &#96;Dial(SIP/1001,...)&#96; or &#96;GoSub(...,SipExtension,...)&#96; for a **single** agent. Replace that with the three lines above (with the right agent id and queue name), or call &#96;Gosub(pbx-phase3-ring-one,s,1(1001,Support))&#96; with your own variables.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Example – replace “SipExtension” with Phase 3 (in us4group.conf or equivalent):**\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nIf you have something like:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\nexten =&gt; SipExtension,1,...\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n,Dial(SIP/${ExtensionNumber},...)\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nyou can change it to:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\nexten =&gt; SipExtension,1,...\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n,Gosub(pbx-phase3-ring-one,s,1(${ExtensionNumber},Inbound))\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n,Return()\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n(Adjust &#96;ExtensionNumber&#96; / queue name to match your variables.)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Step 2.4 – Optional: Recording callback\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nTo send the recording path to the Node app when a call is recorded:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- After **MixMonitor** or **Record**, when you have the file path and the call’s **UniqueID**, call the Recording URL, e.g. from a shell script or from the dialplan:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  &#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\n  Set(PHASE3_REC_PATH=${URIENCODE(${RECORDED_FILE})})\\\\\\\\n  Set(PHASE3_REC_RESULT=${SHELL(curl -s \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"${PHASE3_RECORDING}UniqueID=${UNIQUEID}&amp;Path=${PHASE3_REC_PATH}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")})\\\\\\\\n  &#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- Or from the command line (for testing):\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  &#96;&#96;&#96;bash\\\\\\\\n  curl -s \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"http://YOUR_APP_HOST:3001/api/asterisk/Recording?UniqueID=UNIQUEID&amp;Path=/path/to/file.wav\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n  &#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## 3. Reload and test\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nOn the Asterisk server:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\\\\\\\\nsudo asterisk -rx 'dialplan reload'\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nFrom the Asterisk server, test that it can reach the app:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\\\\\\\\ncurl -s \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"http://YOUR_APP_HOST:3001/api/asterisk/IncomingCall?AgentID=1001&amp;UniqueID=test123&amp;ChannelID=ch1&amp;CustomerChannelID=ch2&amp;CustomerNumber=15551234567&amp;QueueName=Support\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nYou should get a JSON response like &#96;{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"ok\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":true}&#96;.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## 4. File summary\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n| File in repo | Purpose | Where it goes on Asterisk |\\\\\\\\n|--------------|--------|----------------------------|\\\\\\\\n| **phase3-globals.conf** | Reference for globals to add | Copy the lines into &#96;[globals]&#96; in &#96;/etc/asterisk/extensions.conf&#96; |\\\\\\\\n| **phase3-dialplan.conf** | Phase 3 contexts and subroutines | Copy to &#96;/etc/asterisk/phase3-dialplan.conf&#96; and &#96;#include \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"phase3-dialplan.conf\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"&#96; in &#96;extensions.conf&#96;, or paste contents into &#96;extensions.conf&#96; |\\\\\\\\n| **phase3-example-inbound-extension.conf** | Example “inbound to extension” with Phase 3 | Optional; use as reference or include if you want that example |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## 5. PJSIP vs SIP\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThe Phase 3 dialplan uses **PJSIP** (e.g. &#96;Dial(PJSIP/1001,...)&#96;). If your agents are registered as **chan_sip** (SIP), change in **phase3-dialplan.conf**:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- Replace **&#96;PJSIP/&#96;** with **&#96;SIP/&#96;** in the &#96;Dial()&#96; line in &#96;[pbx-phase3-ring-one]&#96;.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"totalLines\\\\\\\":169,\\\\\\\"fileSize\\\\\\\":6080,\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"d:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\web development\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\test\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\docs\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\asterisk\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\README-PHASE3-DIALPLAN.md\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"readRange\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"startLine\\\\\\\":1,\\\\\\\"endLine\\\\\\\":169}}}}}\\\"},\\\"capabilityType\\\":15}\",\"e90433a9-7b93-4912-bd94-5e1a16a17744\":\"{\\\"_v\\\":3,\\\"type\\\":2,\\\"approximateLintErrors\\\":[],\\\"lints\\\":[],\\\"codebaseContextChunks\\\":[],\\\"commits\\\":[],\\\"pullRequests\\\":[],\\\"attachedCodeChunks\\\":[],\\\"assistantSuggestedDiffs\\\":[],\\\"gitDiffs\\\":[],\\\"interpreterResults\\\":[],\\\"images\\\":[],\\\"attachedFolders\\\":[],\\\"attachedFoldersNew\\\":[],\\\"bubbleId\\\":\\\"be59acac-2bc1-4563-98c6-5091dd022aa9\\\",\\\"userResponsesToSuggestedCodeBlocks\\\":[],\\\"suggestedCodeBlocks\\\":[],\\\"diffsForCompressingFiles\\\":[],\\\"relevantFiles\\\":[],\\\"toolResults\\\":[],\\\"notepads\\\":[],\\\"capabilities\\\":[],\\\"multiFileLinterErrors\\\":[],\\\"diffHistories\\\":[],\\\"recentLocationsHistory\\\":[],\\\"recentlyViewedFiles\\\":[],\\\"isAgentic\\\":false,\\\"fileDiffTrajectories\\\":[],\\\"existedSubsequentTerminalCommand\\\":false,\\\"existedPreviousTerminalCommand\\\":false,\\\"docsReferences\\\":[],\\\"webReferences\\\":[],\\\"aiWebSearchResults\\\":[],\\\"requestId\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"attachedFoldersListDirResults\\\":[],\\\"humanChanges\\\":[],\\\"attachedHumanChanges\\\":false,\\\"summarizedComposers\\\":[],\\\"cursorRules\\\":[],\\\"cursorCommands\\\":[],\\\"cursorCommandsExplicitlySet\\\":false,\\\"pastChats\\\":[],\\\"pastChatsExplicitlySet\\\":false,\\\"contextPieces\\\":[],\\\"editTrailContexts\\\":[],\\\"allThinkingBlocks\\\":[],\\\"diffsSinceLastApply\\\":[],\\\"deletedFiles\\\":[],\\\"supportedTools\\\":[],\\\"tokenCount\\\":{\\\"inputTokens\\\":0,\\\"outputTokens\\\":0},\\\"attachedFileCodeChunksMetadataOnly\\\":[],\\\"consoleLogs\\\":[],\\\"uiElementPicked\\\":[],\\\"isRefunded\\\":false,\\\"knowledgeItems\\\":[],\\\"documentationSelections\\\":[],\\\"externalLinks\\\":[],\\\"projectLayouts\\\":[],\\\"unifiedMode\\\":2,\\\"capabilityContexts\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"createdAt\\\":\\\"2026-03-10T10:10:34.971Z\\\",\\\"mcpDescriptors\\\":[],\\\"workspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"conversationState\\\":{\\\"rootPromptMessagesJson\\\":[],\\\"turnsOld\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"pendingToolCalls\\\":[],\\\"turns\\\":[],\\\"previousWorkspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"fileStates\\\":{},\\\"summaryArchives\\\":[],\\\"turnTimings\\\":[],\\\"fileStatesV2\\\":{},\\\"subagentStates\\\":{},\\\"selfSummaryCount\\\":0,\\\"readPaths\\\":[]},\\\"text\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"toolFormerData\\\":{\\\"toolCallId\\\":\\\"tool_4f61841f-7cb4-4587-9924-0746cea36b9\\\",\\\"toolIndex\\\":0,\\\"modelCallId\\\":\\\"tool_4f61841f-7cb4-4587-9924-0746cea36b9\\\",\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\",\\\"name\\\":\\\"read_file_v2\\\",\\\"rawArgs\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"d:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\web development\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\test\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\docs\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\asterisk\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\PHASE3-USAGE-ELABORATED.md\\\\\\\"}\\\",\\\"tool\\\":40,\\\"params\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"targetFile\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"d:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\web development\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\test\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\docs\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\asterisk\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\PHASE3-USAGE-ELABORATED.md\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"charsLimit\\\\\\\":1000000,\\\\\\\"effectiveUri\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"d:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\web development\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\test\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\docs\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\asterisk\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\PHASE3-USAGE-ELABORATED.md\\\\\\\"}\\\",\\\"result\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"contents\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"# Point 3 – Using Phase 3 when ringing an agent (elaborated)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThis note explains **where** in your dialplan “ringing an agent” happens and **how** to use Phase 3 there so the Node app gets IncomingCall, CallAnswered, and CallHangup and the agent dashboard updates in real time.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## What “ringing an agent” means\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n“Ringing an agent” = the moment you **dial the agent’s phone/extension** (e.g. 1001) so the **customer** (inbound caller) is waiting for the agent to answer.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nIn your current dialplan that happens in two main ways:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n1. **Inbound → single extension**  \\\\\\\\n   Call goes to one specific extension (e.g. DID → extension 1001).  \\\\\\\\n   In **us4group.conf** this is **&#96;exten =&gt; SipExtension&#96;** (when &#96;Destination_Action = 9&#96; in WhereToGO).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n2. **Inbound → queue**  \\\\\\\\n   Call enters a queue and Asterisk’s **Queue()** app rings multiple members (e.g. 1001, 1002).  \\\\\\\\n   In **us4group.conf** this is **&#96;exten =&gt; Queue&#96;** with &#96;Queue(${QueueName}, ...)&#96;.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nPhase 3’s **&#96;pbx-phase3-ring-one&#96;** subroutine is for **one agent at a time**. You call it wherever you would otherwise do a single **&#96;Dial(…/agent_extension,…)&#96;** for that inbound customer.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Scenario A – Inbound to single extension (SipExtension)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nHere the call is sent to **one** extension (e.g. 1001). That is “ringing one agent”.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Where it is today\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nIn **us4group.conf** (or wherever you have the same logic), something like:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\nexten =&gt; SipExtension,1,Set(ExtensionInformation=${SHELL(curl \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"${US4GROUP_GetExtensionDetail}ExtensionID=${Destination_Value}&amp;UserID=${UserID}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")})\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n,Set(LastLocation=SipExtension)\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n,...\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n,Set(AgentNumber=${ExtensionNumber})\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n,Set(AgentCallStartTime=${EPOCH})\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n,MixMonitor(${RecPath}${RecName}.wav,b)\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n,Dial(SIP/${ExtensionNumber},${ExtensionTimeout},tTU(InAnswerTime^${CDRID}))\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n,Hangup()\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n(hang),HangUp()\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThe line that actually “rings the agent” is:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\nsame =&gt; n,Dial(SIP/${ExtensionNumber},${ExtensionTimeout},tTU(InAnswerTime^${CDRID}))\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nSo **“using Phase 3 when ringing an agent”** here means: **instead of that &#96;Dial(...)&#96; (and the hangup that follows), call Phase 3 so it can send IncomingCall/CallAnswered/CallHangup to the Node app and still ring the same extension.**\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### What to change (paste this)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Option 1 – Replace only the Dial with Phase 3 (keep your API/MixMonitor if you want)**\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- **File:** &#96;us4group.conf&#96; (or the file that contains &#96;SipExtension&#96;).\\\\\\\\n- **Find:** the &#96;Dial(SIP/${ExtensionNumber},...)&#96; line and the &#96;Hangup()&#96; right after it.\\\\\\\\n- **Replace** that Dial + Hangup with a **Gosub** to Phase 3, then Hangup.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nExample:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Before:**\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n,Set(AgentNumber=${ExtensionNumber})\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n,Set(AgentCallStartTime=${EPOCH})\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n,MixMonitor(${RecPath}${RecName}.wav,b)\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n,Dial(SIP/${ExtensionNumber},${ExtensionTimeout},tTU(InAnswerTime^${CDRID}))\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n,Hangup()\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**After (using Phase 3):**\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n,Set(AgentNumber=${ExtensionNumber})\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n,Set(AgentCallStartTime=${EPOCH})\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n,MixMonitor(${RecPath}${RecName}.wav,b)\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n,Gosub(pbx-phase3-ring-one,s,1(${ExtensionNumber},Inbound))\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n,Hangup()\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n(hang),HangUp()\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- **First argument** &#96;ExtensionNumber&#96; = agent extension (e.g. 1001). Phase 3 will ring that (e.g. &#96;PJSIP/1001&#96; or &#96;SIP/1001&#96;).\\\\\\\\n- **Second argument** &#96;Inbound&#96; = queue/label name for the UI (e.g. “Inbound”). You can use &#96;${QueueName}&#96; or a fixed string like &#96;Support&#96; if you prefer.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nSo in **point 3**, “paste” means: in the **SipExtension** block, **replace the single &#96;Dial(SIP/...)&#96; + &#96;Hangup()&#96;** with **&#96;Gosub(pbx-phase3-ring-one,s,1(${ExtensionNumber},Inbound))&#96;** then **&#96;Hangup()&#96;**, as above.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### If you use PJSIP\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nPhase 3 dialplan uses **PJSIP** by default. If your agents are registered as **SIP** (chan_sip), either:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- Change in **phase3-dialplan.conf** the &#96;Dial(PJSIP/${ARG1},30,...)&#96; line to &#96;Dial(SIP/${ARG1},30,...)&#96;, or  \\\\\\\\n- Keep PJSIP and ensure 1001/1002 are in **pjsip.conf** (or equivalent).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Scenario B – Inbound to queue (Queue())\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nHere the call is put in a **queue** and **Queue()** rings multiple members. The “ringing” is done inside the Queue() app, so you don’t have a single “Dial(agent)” line to replace.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Why Phase 3 “ring one” doesn’t fit Queue() directly\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- **Phase 3** expects: **one** customer channel, **one** agent extension; you call **Gosub(pbx-phase3-ring-one,s,1(agent_id,queue_name))** and it does Dial + callbacks.\\\\\\\\n- **Queue()** does: “ring many members, answer when one answers” and only gives you a macro/URL when the call is **answered** (e.g. SendResponse), not “when we start ringing agent 1001”.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nSo you **cannot** “paste” Phase 3 **inside** Queue() without changing the flow.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Two ways to use Phase 3 with a “queue”\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Option B1 – Keep Queue() for routing; add Phase 3 only when one agent is chosen**\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- Keep your current **Queue()** and **SendResponse** as they are (SendResponse runs when an agent answers).\\\\\\\\n- In **SendResponse** you already notify the old app (AgentOnCall). You can **also** call the Node app’s **CallAnswered** (and optionally **IncomingCall** for the ring state) from that same macro if you pass the right data (agent id, uniqueid, etc.). That would require adding a small curl in SendResponse to your Node **CallAnswered** (and optionally **IncomingCall**) endpoint with the same parameters as in Phase 3.\\\\\\\\n- So “using Phase 3 when ringing an agent” in a queue setup can mean: **when the agent answers**, call **CallAnswered** (and, if you want ring state, **IncomingCall** when the member is tried). The **ring-one** subroutine is not used here; only the **HTTP callbacks** (and your Node app) are used.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Option B2 – Replace Queue() with “try agents in order” using Phase 3**\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- Remove the **Queue()** call for that queue.\\\\\\\\n- Implement “try agent 1001, then 1002, then …” yourself with a loop, and each time you try an agent you call **Gosub(pbx-phase3-ring-one,s,1(agent_id,queue_name))**.\\\\\\\\n- Then “using Phase 3 when ringing an agent” means: **each** time you ring the next agent, you paste that **Gosub** in the loop (see below).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nExample of a **simple** “try two agents in order” using Phase 3 (you can extend with more agents or get the list from an API):\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\n; Replace \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Queue\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" for this queue with a custom “try agents” loop\\\\\\\\nexten =&gt; Queue,1,NoOp(Phase3 try agents in order)\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n,Set(LastLocation=Queue)\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n,Set(PHASE3_QUEUE_NAME=${QueueName})\\\\\\\\n ; Try agent 1001 first\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n,Gosub(pbx-phase3-ring-one,s,1(1001,${PHASE3_QUEUE_NAME}))\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n,GotoIf($[\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"${DIALSTATUS}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" = \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"ANSWER\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"]?Queue,HangME)\\\\\\\\n ; Try agent 1002 next\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n,Gosub(pbx-phase3-ring-one,s,1(1002,${PHASE3_QUEUE_NAME}))\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n,GotoIf($[\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"${DIALSTATUS}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" = \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"ANSWER\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"]?Queue,HangME)\\\\\\\\n ; No one answered\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n(Queue,HangME),Hangup()\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nHere, **each** **Gosub(pbx-phase3-ring-one,s,1(1001,...))** and **Gosub(pbx-phase3-ring-one,s,1(1002,...))** is “using Phase 3 when ringing an agent” for that agent.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Summary table – where to paste / what to do\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n| Your flow | Where “ring agent” happens | What to do for Point 3 |\\\\\\\\n|-----------|-----------------------------|--------------------------|\\\\\\\\n| **Inbound → single extension** (SipExtension, Destination_Action=9) | &#96;Dial(SIP/${ExtensionNumber},...)&#96; in **SipExtension** | Replace that Dial (+ Hangup after) with **Gosub(pbx-phase3-ring-one,s,1(${ExtensionNumber},Inbound))** then Hangup. |\\\\\\\\n| **Inbound → queue** (Queue()) | Inside Queue() app | Either: (B1) Only add CallAnswered/IncomingCall from SendResponse; or (B2) Replace Queue() with a loop and call **Gosub(pbx-phase3-ring-one,s,1(agent_id,queue_name))** for each agent you try. |\\\\\\\\n| **Any other “ring one extension”** | Any &#96;Dial(SIP/1001,...)&#96; or &#96;Dial(PJSIP/1001,...)&#96; | Replace that Dial (and following Hangup if any) with **Gosub(pbx-phase3-ring-one,s,1(1001,YourQueueName))** then Hangup. |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Gosub parameters explained\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\nGosub(pbx-phase3-ring-one,s,1(agent_id,queue_name))\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- **pbx-phase3-ring-one** – context in **phase3-dialplan.conf** that does Dial + IncomingCall/CallAnswered/CallHangup.\\\\\\\\n- **s** – extension (start).\\\\\\\\n- **1** – priority.\\\\\\\\n- **agent_id** – extension number to ring (e.g. &#96;1001&#96; or &#96;${ExtensionNumber}&#96;). Must match your PJSIP/SIP endpoint.\\\\\\\\n- **queue_name** – label for the UI (e.g. &#96;Inbound&#96;, &#96;Support&#96;, &#96;${QueueName}&#96;). Shown as “queue” in the agent dashboard.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nUsually the channel will **hang up inside** Phase 3 (agent or customer hung up), so the next line after &#96;Gosub(...)&#96; in your context may not run. If the Gosub ever returns (e.g. in edge cases), **DIALSTATUS** is set (e.g. &#96;ANSWER&#96;, &#96;NOANSWER&#96;, &#96;BUSY&#96;), so you can branch on it if needed.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nSo when you “use Phase 3 when ringing an agent”, you are literally **adding or replacing** the place that rings that agent with this **Gosub** and the two arguments above.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"totalLinesInFile\\\\\\\":169}\\\",\\\"toolCall\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"readToolCall\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"args\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"d:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\web development\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\test\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\docs\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\asterisk\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\PHASE3-USAGE-ELABORATED.md\\\\\\\"},\\\\\\\"result\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"success\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"# Point 3 – Using Phase 3 when ringing an agent (elaborated)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThis note explains **where** in your dialplan “ringing an agent” happens and **how** to use Phase 3 there so the Node app gets IncomingCall, CallAnswered, and CallHangup and the agent dashboard updates in real time.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## What “ringing an agent” means\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n“Ringing an agent” = the moment you **dial the agent’s phone/extension** (e.g. 1001) so the **customer** (inbound caller) is waiting for the agent to answer.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nIn your current dialplan that happens in two main ways:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n1. **Inbound → single extension**  \\\\\\\\n   Call goes to one specific extension (e.g. DID → extension 1001).  \\\\\\\\n   In **us4group.conf** this is **&#96;exten =&gt; SipExtension&#96;** (when &#96;Destination_Action = 9&#96; in WhereToGO).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n2. **Inbound → queue**  \\\\\\\\n   Call enters a queue and Asterisk’s **Queue()** app rings multiple members (e.g. 1001, 1002).  \\\\\\\\n   In **us4group.conf** this is **&#96;exten =&gt; Queue&#96;** with &#96;Queue(${QueueName}, ...)&#96;.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nPhase 3’s **&#96;pbx-phase3-ring-one&#96;** subroutine is for **one agent at a time**. You call it wherever you would otherwise do a single **&#96;Dial(…/agent_extension,…)&#96;** for that inbound customer.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Scenario A – Inbound to single extension (SipExtension)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nHere the call is sent to **one** extension (e.g. 1001). That is “ringing one agent”.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Where it is today\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nIn **us4group.conf** (or wherever you have the same logic), something like:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\nexten =&gt; SipExtension,1,Set(ExtensionInformation=${SHELL(curl \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"${US4GROUP_GetExtensionDetail}ExtensionID=${Destination_Value}&amp;UserID=${UserID}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")})\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n,Set(LastLocation=SipExtension)\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n,...\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n,Set(AgentNumber=${ExtensionNumber})\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n,Set(AgentCallStartTime=${EPOCH})\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n,MixMonitor(${RecPath}${RecName}.wav,b)\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n,Dial(SIP/${ExtensionNumber},${ExtensionTimeout},tTU(InAnswerTime^${CDRID}))\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n,Hangup()\\\\\\\\n same =&gt; n(hang),HangUp()\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThe line that actually “rings the agent” is:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ini\\\\\\\\nsame =&gt; n,Dial(SIP/${ExtensionNumber},${ExtensionTimeout},tTU(InAnswerTime^${CDRID}))\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nSo **“using Phase 3 when ringing an agent”** here means: **instead of that &#96;Dial(...)&#96; (and the hangup that follows), call Phase 3 so it can send IncomingCall/CallAnswered/CallHangup to the Node app and still ring the same extension.**\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### What to change (paste this)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Option 1 – Replace only the Dial with Phase 3 (keep your API/MixMonitor if you want)**\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- **File:** &#96;us4group.conf&#96;
