---

I now have a comprehensive understanding of the entire codebase. Here is the full technical review report.

---

# PBX Call Centre — Full Codebase Technical Review

## Table of Contents
1. [System Architecture Overview](#1-system-architecture-overview)
2. [Backend Analysis](#2-backend-analysis)
3. [Database Analysis](#3-database-analysis)
4. [Asterisk Integration Review](#4-asterisk-integration-review)
5. [Real-Time Monitoring Review](#5-real-time-monitoring-review)
6. [Reporting System Review](#6-reporting-system-review)
7. [Duplicate Code Findings](#7-duplicate-code-findings)
8. [Performance Issues](#8-performance-issues)
9. [Frontend Code Review](#9-frontend-code-review)
10. [UI/UX Improvement Suggestions](#10-uiux-improvement-suggestions)
11. [Security Observations](#11-security-observations)
12. [Scalability Assessment](#12-scalability-assessment)
13. [Feature Enhancement Suggestions](#13-feature-enhancement-suggestions)

---

## 1. System Architecture Overview

| Layer | Technology | Notes |
|-------|-----------|-------|
| Frontend | React 19 + Vite + recharts | Single-page app, dark theme |
| Backend | Node.js / Express (ESM) | REST API + SSE for real-time |
| Database | MySQL (mysql2/promise) | Pool of 10 connections |
| PBX | Asterisk (ARI + HTTP callbacks) | Queue routing via Stasis app |
| Caching | Optional Redis | Wallboard state caching |
| Sessions | Express-session (MemoryStore) | Not production-ready |

**Architecture flow:**

```
Browser → Vite dev proxy → Express API (/api/*) → MySQL
                                               → Asterisk ARI (WebSocket + REST)
                                               → Redis (optional)
Asterisk dialplan → curl → Express /api/asterisk/* (unauthenticated callbacks)
```

**Roles:** SuperAdmin (1), Admin (2), User (3), Campaign (4), Agent (5)

---

## 2. Backend Analysis

### 2.1 Module Inventory (18 server files + 7 route files)

| File | Lines | Purpose | Severity of Issues |
|------|-------|---------|--------------------|
| `server/index.js` | 67 | Bootstrap, middleware, routes | Medium |
| `server/db.js` | 25 | MySQL pool + query helpers | Low |
| `server/auth.js` | 66 | Auth helpers (bcrypt) | Low |
| `server/seed.js` | 92 | DB init + seeding | Low |
| `server/asterisk-ari.js` | 485 | ARI HTTP client | Medium |
| `server/asterisk-config-sync.js` | 635 | Push config to Asterisk | Medium |
| `server/asterisk-events.js` | 11 | **Stub — not implemented** | Info |
| `server/ari-stasis-queue.js` | 1,007 | ARI Stasis queue app | **High** |
| `server/call-handler.js` | 432 | Call state, CDR, broadcasts | Medium |
| `server/realtime.js` | 135 | SSE pub/sub | Medium |
| `server/agent-sessions.js` | 132 | Session/status logging | Low |
| `server/agent-extension-resolver.js` | 52 | Extension→agent mapping | Low |
| `server/queue-strategy.js` | 185 | Queue ring strategies | Medium |
| `server/session-utils.js` | 23 | Force session destroy | Low |
| `server/report-aggregator.js` | 390 | Background stats rollup | Medium |
| `server/redis-wallboard.js` | 88 | Redis cache (optional) | Medium |
| `server/modules.js` | 19 | Role module definitions | None |
| `routes/auth.js` | 166 | Login/logout/password | Low |
| `routes/agent.js` | 975 | Agent dashboard API | Medium |
| `routes/admin.js` | 815 | Admin dashboard API | Medium |
| `routes/asterisk.js` | 691 | Asterisk dialplan callbacks | **High** |
| `routes/superadmin.js` | **2,614** | Full CRUD for all entities | **High** |
| `routes/wallboard.js` | 1,141 | Wallboard + reports API | **High** |
| `routes/reports.js` | 762 | Historical reports API | Medium |

### 2.2 Critical Issues Found

**ISSUE B-1: All queue/call state is held in-memory (`ari-stasis-queue.js`)**
- **Description:** `pendingCustomers`, `activeBridgedCalls`, `agentLoginStasisChannels`, `pendingOutbound`, and `activeOutboundCalls` are all JavaScript `Map` objects. A server restart or crash loses all active call state.
- **Impact:** Critical — active calls are orphaned; no recovery mechanism.
- **Fix:** Persist active call state to Redis or MySQL. At minimum, add a graceful shutdown handler that logs active calls.

**ISSUE B-2: Debug telemetry left in production code (`routes/wallboard.js`)**
- **Description:** Five `fetch('http://127.0.0.1:7408/ingest/...')` calls on lines 467, 761, 869, 908, and 1007 send internal data to a hardcoded debug endpoint.
- **Impact:** High — leaks internal data, adds latency to every wallboard request, will cause connection-refused errors.
- **Fix:** Remove all five debug fetch calls immediately.

**ISSUE B-3: Asterisk callback routes have zero authentication (`routes/asterisk.js`)**
- **Description:** All endpoints under `/api/asterisk/*` are unauthenticated. If the server is publicly accessible, anyone can forge `AgentLogin`, `IncomingCall`, `CallHangup`, etc.
- **Impact:** High — an attacker can manipulate agent states, create fake call records, or disrupt call routing.
- **Fix:** Add a shared secret header (e.g., `X-Asterisk-Key`) validated in middleware, or restrict to Asterisk server IP via firewall/middleware.

**ISSUE B-4: `superadmin.js` is 2,614 lines — unmaintainable monolith**
- **Description:** Contains CRUD for users, tenants, extensions, trunks, campaigns, inbound routes, outbound routes, queues, IVR menus, time conditions, sounds, voicemail, blacklist, CDR, live agents, Asterisk sync, ARI debug, and logs — all in one file.
- **Impact:** Medium — difficult to review, test, or modify safely.
- **Fix:** Split into sub-routers: `routes/superadmin/users.js`, `routes/superadmin/queues.js`, `routes/superadmin/asterisk-sync.js`, etc.

**ISSUE B-5: Silent error swallowing with `.catch(() => {})`**
- **Description:** Found **72+ instances** across the server codebase of `.catch(() => {})` that silently discard errors. Key offenders: `ari-stasis-queue.js` (26), `wallboard.js` (8), `agent.js` (3), `report-aggregator.js` (4).
- **Impact:** Medium — failures in call record updates, Redis writes, ARI commands, and agent status changes are invisible.
- **Fix:** At minimum, log the error: `.catch(e => console.error('[context]', e.message))`. For critical paths (call records), add retry logic.

**ISSUE B-6: `queue-strategy.js` loads ALL agent_status rows without tenant filter**
- **Description:** `SELECT agent_id, extension_number, status FROM agent_status` fetches every agent across all tenants.
- **Impact:** Performance degrades linearly with total agent count.
- **Fix:** Add `WHERE tenant_id = ?` filter, passing the tenant from the queue context.

**ISSUE B-7: Race conditions in `call-handler.js`**
- **Description:** `updateCallRecordEnd` does `SELECT` then `UPDATE` in two separate queries without a transaction.
- **Impact:** Under high call volume, concurrent hangup events could cause incorrect duration calculations.
- **Fix:** Combine into a single atomic `UPDATE ... SET duration_sec = TIMESTAMPDIFF(...)` or wrap in a transaction.

### 2.3 Naming and Convention Issues

- **`agent_id` vs `agent_user_id`:** The codebase uses `agent_id` (which is `phone_login_number`, a string like "1001") and `agent_user_id` (which is `users.id`, an integer). This dual identity is confusing and error-prone. Multiple JOINs rely on `users.phone_login_number = agent_status.agent_id`.
- **`parseAgentId` in `routes/asterisk.js`** converts string agent IDs to integers with `parseInt`, but they're used as strings elsewhere.
- **No consistent error response format:** Some routes return `{ error: '...' }`, others return `{ message: '...' }`, and some return raw strings.

---

## 3. Database Analysis

### 3.1 Schema Overview (30+ tables)

Core tables: `users`, `tenants`, `agent_status`, `call_records`, `agent_sessions`, `agent_status_log`, `queues`, `queue_members`, `sip_extensions`, `blacklist`, `agent_daily_stats`, `agent_hourly_stats`, `agent_break_stats`, `queue_daily_stats`

### 3.2 Critical Issues

**ISSUE D-1: No foreign keys anywhere in the schema**
- **Description:** Not a single `FOREIGN KEY` constraint is defined. Referential integrity relies entirely on application code.
- **Impact:** High — orphaned rows accumulate over time (e.g., deleting a user leaves behind `agent_status`, `call_records`, `agent_sessions` rows).
- **Fix:** Add FK constraints with `ON DELETE CASCADE` or `ON DELETE SET NULL` where appropriate. At minimum, add FKs for: `call_records.agent_user_id → users.id`, `agent_status.agent_id`, `queue_members.queue_name → queues.name`.

**ISSUE D-2: Missing composite indexes for common query patterns**
- **Description:** Queries frequently filter by `(tenant_id, start_time)`, `(tenant_id, agent_id, start_time)`, and `(tenant_id, status)` but only single-column indexes exist.
- **Impact:** Full table scans on `call_records` for reporting queries, worsening with data growth.
- **Fix:** Add these composite indexes:

```sql
CREATE INDEX idx_cr_tenant_start ON call_records (tenant_id, start_time);
CREATE INDEX idx_cr_tenant_agent_start ON call_records (tenant_id, agent_id, start_time);
CREATE INDEX idx_cr_tenant_status ON call_records (tenant_id, status);
CREATE INDEX idx_users_phone_role ON users (phone_login_number, role);
CREATE INDEX idx_sab_tenant_agent_start ON session_agent_breaks (tenant_id, agent_id, start_time);
```

**ISSUE D-3: `call_records.unique_id` should be UNIQUE, not just indexed**
- **Description:** `idx_unique_id` is a regular index. The code does `INSERT IGNORE INTO call_records ... ON DUPLICATE KEY` relying on uniqueness, but the DDL only has a regular index.
- **Impact:** Potential duplicate call records if the index doesn't enforce uniqueness.
- **Fix:** `ALTER TABLE call_records ADD UNIQUE INDEX uk_unique_id (unique_id);`

**ISSUE D-4: `queue_members` lacks `tenant_id`**
- **Description:** Table has only `queue_name` and `member_name`. Queue names are only unique within a tenant.
- **Impact:** Multi-tenant queue name collisions.
- **Fix:** Add `tenant_id` column and compound unique index `(tenant_id, queue_name, member_name)`.

**ISSUE D-5: Redundant/overlapping migrations**
- **Description:** Migrations 004, 007, and 008 all touch `time_conditions` destination columns. Running them in sequence can cause `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN` errors if columns already exist.
- **Impact:** Migration failures on fresh deployments depending on execution order.
- **Fix:** Use `IF NOT EXISTS` guards or consolidate overlapping migrations.

**ISSUE D-6: `users.parent_id` is confusingly named**
- **Description:** `parent_id` represents `tenant_id` (the owning organization). No FK and no clear naming convention.
- **Impact:** Code readability; new developers will misunderstand the relationship.
- **Fix:** Rename to `tenant_id` or add a comment/alias.

### 3.3 Connection Pool

```javascript
connectionLimit: 10, queueLimit: 0
```

- `connectionLimit: 10` is adequate for development but will bottleneck at 50+ concurrent agents.
- `queueLimit: 0` means unlimited queue — under load, requests queue indefinitely rather than failing fast.
- **Fix:** Set `connectionLimit: 25-50` for production, `queueLimit: 100`, and add connection timeout monitoring.

---

## 4. Asterisk Integration Review

### 4.1 Architecture

Two parallel integration paths exist:

| Path | Mechanism | Used For |
|------|-----------|----------|
| **ARI Stasis** | WebSocket + REST | Queue call routing, agent login, bridges, barge/listen |
| **HTTP callbacks** | Asterisk `curl` → Express | Inbound routing, CDR events, recording paths, IVR/time-conditions |

### 4.2 Issues

**ISSUE A-1: `asterisk-events.js` is a stub**
- **Description:** File only logs env vars; no AMI or event subscription implemented.
- **Impact:** Low (functionality handled elsewhere), but the file is misleading.
- **Fix:** Either implement or remove the file.

**ISSUE A-2: Dialplan docs don't match generated dialplan**
- **Description:** `docs/asterisk/callcentre.conf` shows `AgentLogin()` then `Hangup()` (no Stasis), but `asterisk-config-sync.js` generates `Stasis(queue-dashboard, login, AgentNumber)` after login. The app requires Stasis-based login.
- **Impact:** Anyone following the docs will get a non-functional setup.
- **Fix:** Update `callcentre.conf` docs to match the sync-generated dialplan.

**ISSUE A-3: No curl timeout in Asterisk dialplan**
- **Description:** Dialplan uses `SHELL(curl -s ...)` with no `--connect-timeout` or `--max-time`.
- **Impact:** If the Node server is down, Asterisk calls hang indefinitely at the curl step.
- **Fix:** Add `--connect-timeout 3 --max-time 5` to all curl commands in the generated dialplan.

**ISSUE A-4: ARI error handling returns `{ status: 0 }`**
- **Description:** `ariRequest` catches all errors and returns `{ status: 0 }`. Callers check `status >= 200 && status < 300` but some callers don't check at all.
- **Impact:** Failed ARI operations (hangup, bridge, originate) silently succeed from the app's perspective.
- **Fix:** Throw errors from `ariRequest` and let callers handle them explicitly.

**ISSUE A-5: `queue-failover` PJSIP endpoint uses dummy contact**
- **Description:** `contact=sip:127.0.0.1:9` is used for redirect compatibility.
- **Impact:** Low — works for its purpose but fragile if Asterisk config validation changes.
- **Fix:** Document this hack clearly in the codebase.

---

## 5. Real-Time Monitoring Review

### 5.1 Architecture

- **Agent events:** SSE via `realtime.js` — agents subscribe by `agentUserId`
- **Wallboard events:** SSE via `realtime.js` — supervisors subscribe by `tenantId`
- **Heartbeat:** Every 25 seconds
- **State cache:** Optional Redis (`redis-wallboard.js`)

### 5.2 Issues

**ISSUE R-1: SSE is per-process, doesn't scale horizontally**
- **Description:** `clients` and `wallboardClients` are in-memory Maps. Multiple Node processes (PM2 cluster, load balancer) won't share SSE connections.
- **Impact:** Agents connected to process A won't receive events from process B.
- **Fix:** Use Redis pub/sub as a message bus between processes, or use sticky sessions with a single process per tenant.

**ISSUE R-2: No SSE backpressure handling**
- **Description:** `res.write()` is called without checking if the write buffer is draining. Slow clients accumulate data in Node's write buffer.
- **Impact:** Memory growth under load with many slow clients.
- **Fix:** Check `res.writableLength` before writing; drop events for slow clients or disconnect them.

**ISSUE R-3: `redis.keys()` used in `getAllAgentStates`**
- **Description:** `redis.keys(pattern)` blocks the Redis event loop scanning all keys.
- **Impact:** At 200+ agents, this becomes a performance bottleneck on the Redis server.
- **Fix:** Replace with `redis.scanIterator()` (async iterator) or maintain a Redis SET of active agent keys.

**ISSUE R-4: Wallboard summary triggers excessive fetches**
- **Description:** In `Wallboard.jsx`, every SSE message triggers `loadSummary()`, which makes a full API call. Combined with 2-second polling, this doubles request volume.
- **Impact:** Unnecessary server load; 50 supervisors = 50 extra API calls per event.
- **Fix:** Debounce `loadSummary` (e.g., 3-5 seconds) or have SSE events include the updated data directly.

### 5.3 Capacity Estimate for 100+ Agents

| Component | Current Limit | Bottleneck |
|-----------|--------------|------------|
| SSE connections | ~1,000 (single process) | OK for 200 agents |
| Redis keys scan | Degrades at 500+ keys | Use SCAN |
| MySQL pool (10 conn) | ~50 concurrent queries | Increase to 25-50 |
| Report aggregator | N+1 queries per agent | Batch queries |
| Wallboard polling | 1 request/2s per viewer | Use SSE-only updates |

---

## 6. Reporting System Review

### 6.1 Architecture

Two-tier approach:
1. **Raw data:** `call_records`, `agent_sessions`, `agent_status_log`
2. **Pre-aggregated:** `agent_daily_stats`, `agent_hourly_stats`, `agent_break_stats`, `queue_daily_stats` (filled by `report-aggregator.js` every 5 minutes)

### 6.2 Calculation Verification

| Metric | Formula | Correct? | Notes |
|--------|---------|----------|-------|
| Talk time | `SUM(talk_sec)` from `call_records` | Yes | `talk_sec = TIMESTAMPDIFF(answer_time, end_time)` |
| Pause time | `SUM(TIMESTAMPDIFF(start_time, end_time))` from `session_agent_breaks` | Yes | Open breaks use `NOW()` |
| Login time | `SUM(TIMESTAMPDIFF(login_time, logout_time))` from `agent_sessions` | Yes | Open sessions use `NOW()` |
| AHT | `talk_time_sec / calls_handled` | Yes | Falls back to 0 when no calls |
| Occupancy | `talk_time_sec / login_time_sec * 100` | **Partially** | Doesn't account for wrap-up time; true occupancy = (talk + wrap) / (login - pause) |
| Conversion rate | Not calculated | N/A | `sales_count` column exists in `agent_daily_stats` but is always 0 |

**ISSUE RP-1: Occupancy calculation is simplified**
- **Description:** `occupancy = talk_time / login_time * 100`. Standard call-center occupancy is `(talk + wrap + hold) / (login - unavailable)`.
- **Impact:** Occupancy is understated; supervisors see lower numbers than reality.
- **Fix:** Include hold time and wrap-up time in the numerator; subtract pause/break time from denominator.

**ISSUE RP-2: Report aggregator has N+1 query pattern**
- **Description:** For each tenant, the aggregator queries agents, then for each agent queries call records, sessions, and breaks individually.
- **Impact:** With 200 agents across 5 tenants, this is 600+ queries per 5-minute cycle.
- **Fix:** Use batch queries: `SELECT tenant_id, agent_id, SUM(...) FROM call_records WHERE ... GROUP BY tenant_id, agent_id`.

**ISSUE RP-3: `aggregateAgentDailyStats` duplicated in `wallboard.js`**
- **Description:** The wallboard route re-implements the same aggregation logic from `report-aggregator.js` to serve "today" reports.
- **Impact:** Maintenance burden; logic drift between the two implementations.
- **Fix:** Extract shared aggregation function into a module; call from both places.

---

## 7. Duplicate Code Findings

### 7.1 Backend Duplicates

| Duplicate | Locations | Lines Wasted |
|-----------|-----------|-------------|
| `normalizePhoneForBlacklist()` | `routes/agent.js`, `routes/admin.js` | ~15 |
| Force-logout logic | `routes/admin.js`, `routes/wallboard.js`, `routes/agent.js` | ~60 |
| Force-end-break logic | `routes/admin.js`, `routes/wallboard.js` | ~40 |
| Tenant resolution by user | `routes/admin.js`, `routes/wallboard.js`, `routes/reports.js` | ~30 |
| `queryOne('SELECT tenant_id FROM queues WHERE name = ?')` | `ari-stasis-queue.js` (4+ places) | ~20 |
| Daily stats aggregation | `report-aggregator.js`, `routes/wallboard.js` | ~150 |
| Recording stream handler | `routes/admin.js`, `routes/wallboard.js` | ~30 |

### 7.2 Frontend Duplicates

| Duplicate | Locations | Lines Wasted |
|-----------|-----------|-------------|
| `API_BASE` / `api()` / `apiFetch()` | All 8 page components + AuthContext | ~80 |
| `normalizeRole` / `ROLE_IDS` | `AuthContext.jsx`, `ProtectedRoute.jsx` | ~20 |
| `formatDuration` / `formatDurationSec` / `fmtSec` | Agent, Wallboard, Reports, SuperAdmin | ~40 |
| CSV download logic | Admin, SuperAdmin, Wallboard | ~45 |
| `displayName` construction | AuthContext (lines 74 and 114) | ~5 |

### 7.3 Dead / Unused Code

| Item | Location | Type |
|------|----------|------|
| `asterisk-events.js` | `server/` | Stub file — no functionality |
| Debug fetch calls | `routes/wallboard.js` (5 instances) | Left-over debugging code |
| `.agent-btn-answer`, `.agent-btn-reject`, `.agent-incoming-actions` | `Agent.css` | Unused CSS classes |
| `.incoming-placeholder`, `.btn-answer-demo`, `.contact-list`, `.recent-title` | `Agent.css` | Unused CSS classes |
| `.rpt-back` | `Reports.css` | Unused CSS class |
| `user` from `useAuth()` | `Reports.jsx` | Unused import |
| User.jsx action buttons ("View reports", "Contact list") | `User.jsx` | Non-functional stubs |

### 7.4 Recommended Refactoring

1. Create `server/utils/phone.js` — shared `normalizePhoneForBlacklist`
2. Create `server/utils/agent-actions.js` — shared force-logout, force-end-break
3. Create `server/utils/tenant.js` — shared tenant resolution helpers
4. Create `src/utils/api.js` — single `apiFetch` with auth, error handling, base URL
5. Create `src/utils/format.js` — shared `formatDuration`, `fmtSec`, CSV helpers
6. Move `normalizeRole` and `ROLE_IDS` into `AuthContext` and import from there

---

## 8. Performance Issues

### 8.1 Critical Performance Issues

| # | Issue | Location | Impact | Fix |
|---|-------|----------|--------|-----|
| P-1 | `redis.keys()` blocks Redis | `redis-wallboard.js:56` | Blocks all Redis ops | Use `SCAN` iterator |
| P-2 | Agent status loads all rows | `queue-strategy.js` | O(n) with total agents | Add tenant filter |
| P-3 | N+1 queries in aggregator | `report-aggregator.js` | 600+ queries/cycle | Batch GROUP BY |
| P-4 | Sequential DB queries in `setAgentRinging` | `call-handler.js` | 4-5 queries per ring event | Batch or parallelize |
| P-5 | Double-fetch on wallboard (SSE + poll) | `Wallboard.jsx` | 2x API calls | Debounce or SSE-only |
| P-6 | `SuperAdmin.jsx` is 4,888 lines — single component | `SuperAdmin.jsx` | Full re-render on any state change | Split into view components |
| P-7 | Connection pool of 10 | `db.js` | Bottleneck at 50+ agents | Increase to 25-50 |
| P-8 | No query caching for static data | Multiple routes | Repeated identical queries | Cache tenants, queues in memory with TTL |

### 8.2 Recommended Optimizations

**Caching:**
- Cache tenant list, queue list, and extension list in memory with 60-second TTL (these rarely change)
- Use Redis for wallboard state (already implemented, make it the default)
- Cache queue strategy member lists for 5-10 seconds

**Query improvements:**
- Add composite indexes (see Database section)
- Batch aggregation queries with GROUP BY instead of per-agent loops
- Use `EXPLAIN` on reporting queries and add covering indexes

**Background processing:**
- Move call record updates to a write-behind queue (Redis list + worker)
- Run report aggregation as a separate process, not in the API server
- Use database triggers or scheduled events for `agent_daily_stats` rollup

---

## 9. Frontend Code Review

### 9.1 Component Structure

| Component | Lines | Complexity | Needs Refactoring? |
|-----------|-------|-----------|-------------------|
| `SuperAdmin.jsx` | 4,888 | **Very High** — 100+ useState hooks | **Yes — urgent** |
| `Agent.jsx` | 656 | High — SSE, calls, breaks, CRM | Moderate |
| `Wallboard.jsx` | 686 | High — SSE + polling + timers | Moderate |
| `Reports.jsx` | 608 | Medium — 9 tabs, charts | Moderate |
| `Admin.jsx` | 399 | Medium | Low |
| `AgentExtensionSelect.jsx` | 229 | Low | No |
| `Login.jsx` | 73 | Low | No |
| `User.jsx` | 43 | Low | No |
| `Layout.jsx` | 35 | Low | No |
| `ProtectedRoute.jsx` | 39 | Low | No |

### 9.2 State Management

- **No global state library** — all state is local `useState` with prop drilling and context
- `AuthContext` is the only shared context
- Agent state, wallboard state, and report data are all fetched independently in each page
- No state persistence across page navigation (data refetched on every mount)

**Recommendation:** For a production call center, add a lightweight state manager (Zustand is a good fit for React 19) to share agent status and queue data across pages.

### 9.3 Rendering Efficiency

**ISSUE F-1: `SuperAdmin.jsx` re-renders entirely on any state change**
- With 100+ `useState` hooks, changing any value (e.g., toggling a form field) triggers a full re-render of ~4,900 lines of JSX.
- **Fix:** Split into `<UsersView>`, `<QueuesView>`, `<TrunksView>`, etc., each with their own state.

**ISSUE F-2: Wallboard 1-second timer causes frequent re-renders**
- `setInterval` every 1 second updates `now` state, re-rendering the entire wallboard.
- **Fix:** Use `WallboardBreakDuration` pattern (local timer in sub-component) for all ticking durations; avoid updating parent state.

**ISSUE F-3: No `React.memo` or `useMemo` on expensive computations**
- Table rendering, stats calculations, and chart data preparation are all inline.
- **Fix:** Memoize expensive computations with `useMemo`; wrap table rows with `React.memo`.

### 9.4 Code Reuse

- No shared utility module for API calls, formatting, or CSV export
- No shared form components (each page reinvents `<input>`, `<select>`, modal patterns)
- No shared table component (each page writes its own `<table>` with similar styling)

---

## 10. UI/UX Improvement Suggestions

### 10.1 Dashboard Layout

| Issue | Page | Suggestion |
|-------|------|-----------|
| SuperAdmin sidebar is fixed 230px | SuperAdmin | Collapsible sidebar with hamburger menu on mobile |
| No breadcrumb navigation | SuperAdmin | Add breadcrumbs for nested views (Queues → Edit Queue) |
| Dense filter bars | Reports, Wallboard | Use collapsible filter panel or dropdown filters |
| Tables overflow on mobile | All | Add responsive card view alternative for <768px |

### 10.2 Agent Monitoring Pages

| Issue | Suggestion |
|-------|-----------|
| Agent status colors are small badges | Use full-row background tinting for at-a-glance scanning |
| Break duration requires mental math | Show countdown timer and total break time prominently |
| No audio/visual alert for incoming calls | Add browser notification API + audio alert with opt-in |
| Transfer modal has no search | Add searchable extension/queue dropdown |

### 10.3 Reporting Pages

| Issue | Suggestion |
|-------|-----------|
| 9 tabs in a horizontal scroll | Group into categories: Agent Performance, Queue Performance, System |
| Charts lack data labels | Add value labels on bar charts for printing |
| No date preset buttons | Add "Today", "This Week", "This Month", "Last 30 Days" quick filters |
| Charts have no screen reader support | Add `<table>` alternative view for accessibility |
| No export for charts | Add "Export as PNG" for chart images |

### 10.4 Navigation Flow

| Issue | Suggestion |
|-------|-----------|
| Reports page doesn't use shared `Layout` | Wrap in `Layout` for consistent header/logout |
| User.jsx is a stub | Either implement or redirect users to Admin view |
| No "Back to Dashboard" button in Reports | Add consistent navigation back |
| Login page shows dev seed credentials | Remove or hide behind environment flag |

### 10.5 Data Readability

| Issue | Suggestion |
|-------|-----------|
| Large numbers not formatted | Use `toLocaleString()` for numbers > 999 |
| Duration shown as raw seconds in some places | Consistent `HH:MM:SS` or "2h 15m" format everywhere |
| No color coding for performance metrics | Red/yellow/green for SLA, AHT, occupancy thresholds |
| Tables lack sorting | Add client-side column sorting for all data tables |

---

## 11. Security Observations

### 11.1 Critical

| # | Issue | Location | Fix |
|---|-------|----------|-----|
| S-1 | Asterisk callbacks have **no authentication** | `routes/asterisk.js` | Add shared secret header or IP whitelist |
| S-2 | Session secret has insecure default | `server/index.js` | Require `SESSION_SECRET` env var; fail startup if missing |
| S-3 | MemoryStore sessions — no expiry management | `server/index.js` | Use `connect-redis` or `express-mysql-session` |

### 11.2 Medium

| # | Issue | Location | Fix |
|---|-------|----------|-----|
| S-4 | Recording path traversal — basic check only | `routes/admin.js` | Add `path.resolve()` + strict prefix validation |
| S-5 | Seed users with password `demo123` | `seed.js` | Force password change on first login |
| S-6 | CORS_ORIGIN defaults to `localhost:5173` | `.env.example` | Document production CORS configuration |
| S-7 | ARI credentials in `.env.example` | `.env.example` | Remove example credentials (`india@123`) |
| S-8 | No rate limiting on login endpoint | `routes/auth.js` | Add `express-rate-limit` (e.g., 5 attempts/minute) |
| S-9 | No CSRF protection | `server/index.js` | Add `csurf` middleware or SameSite cookie attribute |
| S-10 | No input validation library | All routes | Add Zod or Joi schema validation for all POST bodies |

### 11.3 Low

| # | Issue | Location | Fix |
|---|-------|----------|-----|
| S-11 | `session.destroy()` callback not awaited | `routes/auth.js` | Use promisified version |
| S-12 | Login error reveals account existence | `routes/auth.js` | Use generic "Invalid credentials" message (already does this, but the message "Invalid credentials or account disabled" leaks that the account exists and is disabled) |

### 11.4 SQL Injection Assessment

**All database queries use parameterized queries (`?` placeholders).** No raw string concatenation into SQL was found. This is well-implemented.

### 11.5 XSS Assessment

- React's JSX auto-escapes by default — no `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` usage found
- Server responses are JSON — no HTML rendering server-side
- **Risk is low** but input validation (S-10) would add defense-in-depth

---

## 12. Scalability Assessment

### 12.1 Current Limits

| Component | Current Capacity | 200+ Agent Target | Gap |
|-----------|-----------------|-------------------|-----|
| MySQL pool | 10 connections | ~50 needed | **Increase to 25-50** |
| SSE connections | ~1,000/process | 200 agents + 20 supervisors | OK (single process) |
| In-memory call state | Unlimited but volatile | 200 concurrent calls | **Add persistence** |
| Report aggregator | O(n) queries per agent | 200 agents = 600 queries/cycle | **Batch queries** |
| Redis (optional) | keys() blocks at 500+ | 200 keys acceptable | **Use SCAN** |
| Session store | MemoryStore — single process | Doesn't scale | **Use Redis store** |
| Asterisk ARI WebSocket | 1 connection | 1 is sufficient | OK |
| Frontend bundle | ~4,900 line SuperAdmin | Slow initial load | **Code-split** |

### 12.2 Scaling Recommendations

**Phase 1 (supports 200 agents):**
1. Increase MySQL `connectionLimit` to 30
2. Replace `MemoryStore` with `connect-redis`
3. Batch report-aggregator queries
4. Add composite database indexes
5. Add curl timeouts to Asterisk dialplan

**Phase 2 (supports 500+ agents):**
1. Persist call state to Redis (not just in-memory Maps)
2. Separate report aggregation into a dedicated worker process
3. Add Redis pub/sub for SSE across multiple Node processes
4. Implement database partitioning on `call_records` by month
5. Add read replicas for reporting queries

**Phase 3 (supports 1,000+ agents):**
1. Horizontal scaling with load balancer + sticky sessions
2. Separate API server from ARI Stasis server
3. Event-driven architecture (RabbitMQ/Kafka) for call events
4. Time-series database (TimescaleDB) for metrics
5. CDN for frontend assets

---

## 13. Feature Enhancement Suggestions

### 13.1 Monitoring Improvements

- **Agent screen pop:** Show CRM customer details automatically when call connects (data is in `crm_customers`, just needs frontend integration)
- **Queue SLA alerts:** Real-time notification when queue wait time exceeds threshold
- **Agent activity timeline:** Visual timeline showing status changes throughout the day
- **Heat map:** Show call volume by hour/day in a heat map on wallboard

### 13.2 Reporting Analytics

- **Conversion tracking:** The `sales_count` column exists but is never populated — add a disposition/outcome selection after each call
- **First call resolution (FCR):** Track whether callers call back within X hours
- **Abandon rate trends:** Chart abandon rates by time-of-day to identify staffing gaps
- **Agent comparison:** Side-by-side comparison of any 2-5 agents with radar charts (partially exists)
- **Scheduled reports:** Email daily/weekly PDF reports to supervisors

### 13.3 Supervisor Tools

- **Silent monitoring improvements:** Add whisper mode UI indicator and coach mode
- **Queue rebalancing:** Drag-and-drop agents between queues in real-time
- **Break approval workflow:** Supervisors approve/deny break requests before agents go on break
- **Sticky notes / agent annotations:** Supervisors can attach notes to agents visible on wallboard
- **Shift scheduling:** Basic shift calendar to know expected vs. actual agent availability

### 13.4 UI Improvements

- **Dark/Light theme toggle:** Currently hardcoded dark theme
- **Customizable wallboard:** Drag-and-drop widgets for metrics, charts, agent lists
- **Mobile-responsive agent view:** Agents should be able to take calls from a mobile browser
- **Keyboard shortcuts:** Ctrl+A to answer, Ctrl+H to hangup, Ctrl+T to transfer
- **Toast notifications:** Replace `alert()` calls with toast notifications (currently no toasts)

---

| B-3 | Add auth to Asterisk callback routes | Security |
| S-2 | Require strong `SESSION_SECRET` | Security |
| S-8 | Add rate limiting to login | Security |
| D-3 | Make `call_records.unique_id` UNIQUE | Data integrity |

| 7.4 | Create shared utility modules (frontend + backend) | Maintainability |

---

This report covers all 13 requested areas. The system is functionally solid with good Asterisk integration, but needs security hardening (especially Asterisk callback auth and session management), performance optimization (indexes, batching, connection pool), and significant refactoring of the two monolith files (`superadmin.js` at 2,614 lines and `SuperAdmin.jsx` at 4,888 lines). The reporting foundation is strong but the occupancy calculation needs correction and the aggregator needs query batching for scale.
